We didn’t know whether he was detained or relocated, but we never saw him again after that If I wanted to go to the toilets When they tried to bargain with me They bargained with me a lot, even the well-known emirs There were prominent figures among them Specifically, one from the al-Amer family and one from the al-Mukhtar family bargained with me They told me, “Bring us fertilizer” Fertilizer 33, the one they use to make explosives They bargained with me to bring cigarettes and hide the fertilizer inside them for them Many people bargained with me, not just them Even Abu Seif himself bargained with me to work with him And to pass on reports and information to him He told me, “Whatever you want in return, you’ll get it” I told him, “I’m not that kind of man” If I wanted to go to the toilets One day, the organization took me Abu Seif came to me. The first time, he came with Abu Khaled al-Idlibi and other men They took me away They said, “Come with us to carry out your sentence” Of course They took me along with six other men Among them was a man named Abdul Karim al-Ghaffaf He was from the al-Marandi clan He was one of my uncles, and his charge was adopting Shia beliefs An old man, about 80 years old There was also another man from the al-Bu Assaf clan named Yasin al-Yasin Anyway There were six men, and I was the seventh They took me in a car, and I felt that we had left the city They made us get out, and there was a cool breeze in the air They made us kneel on our knees And when they made us kneel One of the emirs said, “Remove the cloth blindfold from Talal al-Shuweimi’s eyes” They removed the blindfold from my eyes Then I looked to my right and left and saw that everyone was lined up on both sides They told them, “You did this and that, say the two testimonies” So they recited the testimonies, and each one was shot in the head I looked around and the place was full of trees,it used to be a Baath Pioneers camp When we were kids, we used to go there in summer for school camps during the Assad regime One of them said to me, “Talal al-Shuweimi” What do you want? I said, I don’t want anything. You brought me here and sentenced me to execution He said to me They removed the cloth blindfold from my eyes so I could see around me Among them were well-known people,some of the senior figures in the ISIS state High-ranking figures who are still alive today He said to me Now we’ll carry out the sentence and get rid of you Then another masked man came And said to me What is your last wish? At that moment I told him, to speak with my children He placed the pistol on my head He said, “Say the two testimonies,” so I did Then he struck me As if he hit me in the head, but he fired the shot beside my ear When he fired beside my ear, out of terror and horror My hands were tied behind my back, and I fell to the ground From the terror of it,it was like an American movie I mean, something Something indescribable, something even the Jews wouldn’t envy you for Nor the Christians, nor Israel, nor even black devils would wish to see you in such a position They wouldn’t even gloat over you The last time I was imprisoned,as I said earlier,they transferred me from one prison to another To a prison hidden in a corner Sadly, that prison was across from my mother’s house My mother’s name is Zahia And one of the emirs, named Muhammad al-Abadi Used to tell me, “Zahia’s son, I saw Zahia walking in the garden” We were on the second floor, and he said, “I saw your mother” The man was joking with me We escaped from the prison during the days of the Euphrates Dam collapse in Raqqa There were several of us, and that same man was with us His name was Abu Muhammad al-Abadi May God bless and reward him We made a hole in the prison wall He escaped with us, helped us, and watched the road. There were seven of us, and a car was waiting We escaped by car, the seven of us As for me, there’s a saying: they asked the nail, “Why did you go into the wall?” He answered, “From how much they hit my head” I didn’t sell cigarettes by choice, but I was an employee My salary was 12,000 Syrian Lira, working in the Water Authority After I started working, Air Force Intelligence raided my house and detained my brother, taking him to Sednaya Prison When they raided my place and I fled, I had 10 children They needed food and bread I bought a taxi without papers and drove it, but the income wasn’t enough And cigarette trade wasn’t punishable by execution or anything like that But the organization imposed execution on us, even though it wasn’t for cigarette trade Their judgments became vindictive They invented charges,collaboration, cigarettes, ammunition,just to sentence us to execution I was forced into cigarette trade. I have children and pay rent for two houses Every day I’d buy bread for 2,000 or 3,000 Syrian Lira, and there was no other work With the organization, either you join them or remain jobless So how could I feed my children? Should I trade cigarettes to earn a living and feed them, or let them starve to death? Just like many others, there were about 2,000 people And I was under a death sentence Prison had a huge impact on me When I first escaped from prison I started crying Tears of joy, not sadness I began kissing the ground I looked up to God Almighty, crying and shouting, breathing in the air So many things happened to me Prison affected me deeply,like forgetfulness, for example It left me with memory loss Tragic events that scarred me In my life, I never held hatred toward anyone But they made my heart hard They killed the mercy inside me as a human being belonging to humanity They ignored so many rights,human rights, and my rights as a Muslim As a father and provider for a family, for example They stripped the mercy from within me There are still many things inside me ISIS affected me deeply, and I still suffer from what’s called forgetfulness On top of that, the greatest hardship remains,that only God provides and sustains us God Almighty is the Provider For me, ISIS destroyed my life Why am I still struggling today to buy bread? Why am I looking for a job as a taxi driver for daily pay? What’s the reason? Because of an ignorant man Because of an infidel Who should be in prison for two or three years God willing, may the Almighty take from my life And give it to his Just so I live long enough until he gets out of prison, to see him even for one hour To settle my score with him An eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth, and the aggressor is the unjust one Everyone dies once Better that than dying every hour before your children
full_text_by_file.01_تعريف-ملخص القصة_En.srt: My name is Talal Mustafa al-Shuweimi I’m from Raqqa Province, living on Sajiyah Street I’m 45 years old I have 3 wives and 13 children During the Assad regime’s control of Raqqa, I worked at the Water Department After the problems and battles started I was dismissed from work The regime accused me of being a terrorist The Air Force Intelligence raided my house Because they claimed I was a terrorist The dismissal order came from Damascus Of course It was all a malicious report Because we were locals and stayed in the city My dismissal order came in 2012 So I fled Raqqa for a while And my brother was arrested By the Assad regime On behalf of the regime They put him in Sednaya Prison for about 6 years After they detained my brother for 6 years in prison The Free Syrian Army entered Raqqa I was already dismissed from work as a “terrorist” About 6 or 7 months after the FSA entered ISIS entered the city, calling itself the Islamic State They took control of the area Within their first 2 or 3 months of settling in They arrested me, accusing me of being a regime agent,a shabiha [pro-regime thug] They detained me at Point 11, with the security members Inside the former Governor’s Palace I stayed there for about a week After the Governor’s Palace, they took me To a new security base near the National Hospital area in Raqqa It was inside the old Military Judiciary building of the Assad regime A week later, they moved me from the Governor’s Palace basement To the old Military Judiciary building near the Traffic Circle I stayed in that base Under the same charge, regime agent I stayed there for 53 days After 53 days They released me because there was no evidence against me The one who interrogated me Was from Raqqa Province, from Tabqa city His family name was Khameesi He was called Abu Yousuf, the interrogator He was the head of interrogators Another man was nicknamed Abu Abdurrahman His name was Mahmoud al-Ali al-Sahou from the Ghanem al-Zahir clan From an area called al-Jaif East of the al-Eywij area Near Rashid Farm in Raqqa I stayed under interrogation for about 53 days After they confirmed there was nothing against me They realized I’d already been dismissed by the Assad regime as a “terrorist” So they released me And gave me a clearance card A certificate proving there were no charges against me As a Syrian citizen And a Sunni by faith I went back to work with the other workers Trying to earn a living to support my children Around 50 or 60 days after returning I was surprised to see someone Sadly, he was from the same neighborhood, Sunni, and a local of the area His name was Mahmoud al-Shahada He was an ISIS emir overseeing the Water Department His name was Mahmoud al-Shahada Known as Abu Hudhayfa Abu Hudhayfa had been detained in Assad regime prisons back in 2000 Accused of joining Jund al-Sham He struck a deal with the regime in Sednaya Prison and was released Then brought to Raqqa and appointed as an emir in the caliphate Mahmoud al-Shahada, known as Abu Hudhayfa, and his brother were both active and skilled His brother’s name was Hassan He had two brothers, Hassan and Hassaan His brother was an emir and his right-hand man When I came back to the Water Department I was surprised he’d become the department’s emir after being released from Sednaya When the workers learned I was coming to work again I had no salary, but people gave us charity for the children They offered us odd jobs outside Of course, we had no fixed income since I and others were dismissed from work Then Abu Hudhayfa came And arrested me himself,Mahmoud al-Shahada By then, he’d become “Minister of the Caliphate” because of his influence and knowledge He had many initiatives, so he arrested me He tricked me into it At the time, we were fixing a broken water line In the Khasra Faraj area, and when we came He said, “The sheikh wants to see you” “He wants to ask a few questions, and there’s also a broken pipe at the security point” He said, “You should fix it.” I said, “As you wish, Abu Hudhayfa” When I arrived there, I only saw weapons pointed at me They threw me to the ground, stepped on my neck, and arrested me For about two months When I returned to work at the Water Department Abu Hudhayfa grabbed me by the hair Dragged me toward the table Pressed my head onto it And placed his foot on my left shoulder He said to me, “You’re a shabih (pro-regime thug). You shouldn’t enter the institution. You’re an agent for the regime” I told him, Calm down, Sheikh, this is my clearance from Point 11, and I’m clean, there’s nothing against me I have children, but he said, No, you’re a shabih And no one will be able to get you back to work We brought mediations from one sheikh and another, but nothing worked At that point, I had a small amount of money And my brother had about 125,000 Syrian pounds So I bought a taxi A taxi with no official papers Back then they used to call it “Arouriya” Because it was stolen,they’d steal and sell it without papers So they called it an “Arouriya car” I worked as a taxi driver For about 5 to 6 months One day The Hisba (religious police) raided me at my home On 16th Street They said to me, You’re a man who trades cigarettes And they detained me for 4 days They insulted me, but it wasn’t severe or extreme They kept saying, You’re a cigarette dealer, and so on I told him, Sheikh, I’m not a cigarette dealer, I don’t even smoke cigarettes, but yes, I smoke hookah Yes, I smoke hookah But I don’t smoke cigarettes. If I did, I’d tell you Anyway, they detained me One man And took me to a Hisba base called “Panorama” It’s in Raqqa, near Maqs Roundabout, called “Panorama” There was a Hisba base there. The emir in charge was Abu Bishr Abu Bishr, a Saudi national After 4 days of detention I moved my home to another neighborhood, fearing someone was targeting me I was hungry, and I had 12 or 13 children There was no strength left in me I thought, There are people who trade cigarettes and make a living from it So I started selling cigarettes. Honestly, the accusation already stuck to me anyway So I decided to trade cigarettes And I began working in that trade For about a month or a month and a half Then a Hisba patrol came and arrested me They detained me for about 15 days And the one who arrested me Was a man from Rumaila named Mustafa al-Hamid al-Muslih Known as Abu Seif Maqs He also had another nom de guerre, Abu Seif Doshka Also known as Abu Seif Maqs He was originally from the Wahb clan His maternal uncles were from the Masaar group of the Barij clan As for Abu Seif Maqs, his past Was extremely bad He used to take hashish and pills and was unemployed And strangely enough When ISIS entered, I was shocked to see he had become a Doshka gunner Then, since he was from the area and knew people They made him emir of the Search and Investigation Unit, this man called Mustafa Mustafa arrested me For the second time, for about 15 days. He tortured, beat, and insulted me Of course, they found no cigarettes with me The emir of the Search Unit was the same Mustafa al-Muslih Known as Abu Seif And another man called Ibrahim al-Ali al-Khayheet From the Ghanem al-Zahir clan, living near the Jazra Junction in Raqqa Known as Abu Hudhayfa Abu Hudhayfa was about 30 years old The sharia official was Abu Hamza al-Tunsi There was another man, originally from Sukhna, living on Sajiyah Street in Raqqa Named Abu Zayd His brother was the emir of the Islamic Police In Raqqa Province, overseeing the whole wilaya Abu Zayd was a sharia official Second in rank under Abu Hamza al-Tunsi He worked as a messenger between the court and the Hisba’s sharia office As for how many times I was imprisoned If I tried to describe my suffering, I’d need 3 months to finish The torture and misery we endured I was imprisoned 28 times Some people might think I’m lying, but the locals know me well My bag was always ready, because every 2 or 3 days ISIS would arrest me The first time I was jailed in a security checkpoint, the charge was suspicion I was imprisoned there for 56 days The other 27 times were all for cigarette trading My detentions ranged between 10 to 15 days, sometimes up to 2 months Then they’d release me, since they found no evidence, and I was very cautious around them
full_text_by_file.02_الاعتقال_En.srt: The last time They Launched a campaign against cigarette sellers, and ISIS members specifically targeted me Me and 2 or 3 other people 7 people, including me, were sentenced to qisas [execution by retribution] They sentenced them to qisas with me too They summoned me and took me by deceit They tricked me and took me away They called me on the phone and said, Are you Talal al-Shuweimi? I said, Yes, I am Talal al-Shuweimi They said, Someone confessed against you He said you sold him 2 cartons of cigarettes That wasn’t true at all Because I hadn’t personally sold cigarettes for about a year Yes, I was a cigarette dealer But When I was in the trade, I didn’t distribute or sell cigarettes myself I didn’t distribute or sell them When they summoned me, I told them, I’ll come right away I got in my car and went to the Hisba, which was at the end of Sajiyah Street On Atiya Bakery Street And the Hisba emir who was on Sajiyah Street Was named Issa, from the Jais clan, known as Abu Humam And Abu Humam, before that Had been a sergeant in the Political Security branch under the Assad regime When he was a sergeant under the Assad regime and ISIS was established Abu Humam came and announced his defection and joined the Islamic State When Abu Humam summoned me, he had already raided my house about 10 times Out of 27 raids, he came several times, knowing they’d never find cigarettes with me One time he knocked on my door and called out, “Hey Shuweimi!” Your hookah is running, and we know you don’t have cigarettes, but bring the hookah and come So I carried the hookah with its coals and placed it in his car When Abu Humam summoned me, I asked him, Where’s the man? Who testified against me? He told me, Rest for 5 minutes Now I’ll call the man When he called me, he said the man is here with me, come see him When I went, it turned out there was no man there at all He said, In 5 minutes he’ll come So I waited 5 minutes And said, Abu Humam, where’s the man? He told me, You sold cigarettes I said, Sheikh I swore to him by God, and honestly, I swear truthfully, I don’t sell cigarettes And I don’t distribute cigarettes. I have workers who sell and deliver instead of me What surprised me was his attitude, and I sat down I told him, Abu Humam, let me tell you something. Even if I sold two boxes of cigarettes You’re not smarter than Abu Seif. If he were in your place, he would’ve arrested me right away The news would’ve reached Abu Seif before it reached you He said, God willing, and started laughing
full_text_by_file.03_سجن التحري_En.srt: They took me to the Investigation Section and up to the second floor They brought me into a room on the second floor, where the Investigation Section was located It was headed by Abu Hamza al-Tunisi, the senior sharia official and overall emir Under him were the Emir of Investigation, Abu Seif Maqs, and Abu Hudhayfa And Abu Zaid, from the Sakhani clan His brother, Fayez, was the Emir of the Islamic Police They took me upstairs My hands were tied behind my back And I was blindfolded, and they began beating me They hit me with sticks and metal rods , the beating was extremely painful I was beaten for 3 days straight They poured water on me and kept beating me again and again When I woke up, I found myself hanging on the wall on the second floor That area was at the start of Sajiyah Street, at the intersection with Hittin School Street That place was known as the Investigation Zone I found myself hanging I remained hanging on the wall for 18 days It was the middle of the hot summer season But our Lord, praise be to Him, is merciful in His mercy And in His glory and majesty The heat actually helped me endure the beatings and pain When my body was hot and sweating, the blows hurt less than when it was cold in winter I suffered terribly from thirst My toes barely touched the ground And I could hardly reach a bottle of drink I would bring it close when they gave me a break to drink, from the intensity of thirst Because of the severe torture Abu Seif wrapped his hand around my beard He grabbed my beard,it was long,and dragged me by it while my hands were shackled I was out of breath, but God Almighty gives a person strength Great and powerful strength He kept dragging me by my beard, saying, I’ll teach the whole town a lesson through you He said to me, plainly, “The time of the Prophet is gone” I told him, Are we now living in Pharaoh’s time? For you to say the Prophet’s era has passed? He dragged me by my beard until he brought me to the feet of Ibrahim al-Fakkash Who was nicknamed al-Bariwi He was sentenced along with me too They told us we’d been sentenced to execution, but the ruling hadn’t been carried out yet He dragged me by my beard, pulling me along the ground Until we reached the feet of Ibrahim al-Fakkash, nicknamed al-Bariwi He asked him, Do you know this man? He replied, Yes, I know him Abu Seif said to al-Bariwi, This is the clever player, al-Shuweimi They called me the clever player because they never caught me with cigarettes I’d been in so many prisons and detentions,arrested 27 times before by the Hisba (religious police) Once they detained me for security reasons, and another time I spent a month and a half in Point 11 That’s why they gave me that nickname He dragged me by my beard all that distance, pulled me out of the room again, still dragging me I stayed with them in the investigation section for about 53 or 54 days They came And shackled my hands behind my back again They took me out after 53 days And said to me We’ll spare your neck if you pay a sum of money In exchange for paying a financial amount The ruling would be treated like theft but under a religious ruling In the past, people would say things like, I’ll rob you right before your eyes My strength and my dignity They wanted to rob me, but in the name of what the Prophet said,meaning freeing a slave They asked me, What property do you have? At that time, I owned 6 or 7 cars Because I worked hard in the cigarette trade Whether that work was forbidden or not Some people said it was forbidden, others said it wasn’t Maybe we were ignorant about it, but we didn’t really know whether it was forbidden or not The important thing is, I worked hard, forbidden or not I struggled and earned that money They took everything I had,gold, silver, and cars They took everything, even my farmland I had a deed of ownership from 50 years ago In my name, inherited from my father Back then, the Syrian regime used to seize land from people who owned a lot of it Out of fear, my father registered the land in his sons’ names He divided the land equally among his sons So my share was the same as my brothers’ Even the land that was originally my father’s They made me fingerprint it as sold and bought I also had a motorbike with two frames at times I used it around the neighborhood and inside the city during traffic jams and hot weather They made me fingerprint that as sold and bought too
full_text_by_file.04_التحقيق_En.srt: I was detained many times, and regarding the interrogation sessions They sometimes lasted long, sometimes they interrogated me, sometimes they didn’t Just to put psychological pressure on me My first interrogation was at Point 11 I talked too much during the interrogation because of how unjustly they treated me I was innocent,I was just trying to earn a living, working as a taxi driver Because I talked so much, they interrogated me and took me to the judge Their system was that every week they brought us a blank sheet We’d write our names, like Talal Mustafa al-Shuweimi Was he interrogated? If yes, he should write “yes” If not interrogated, he should write “no” And was he presented to the judge? “Yes” or “no” And every time, I wrote “no,” blaming them for my case Since I had children, no means, and my house was rented in the beginning Sometimes they’d take me to the prison corridor At Point 11 They’d leave me for two or three hours, blindfolded Standing on my feet Or sitting on the ground They wouldn’t speak to me or beat me But I could hear the sounds of torture and beatings in the corridor They’d shout at the prisoners, Stand up, you dog! Turn around! Lift your foot! They had their own methods of torture But they didn’t beat me, they just kept me in the corridor blindfolded Leaving me that way for two or three hours In the end, he’d say to me, Sheikh Go back to your group cell. I’d remove the blindfold inside and hand it to him through the window That happened about 10 times And the last time I told them I wanted to be interrogated because I was honestly afraid they’d put me in the corridor again And one time I lost my patience I started crying and said, It’s only one death A person only dies once I started crying and shouting, so they blindfolded me and took me away They took me to the interrogation room And the interrogator He died, may God have mercy on him His name was Abu Abdul Rahman He was from the Jaif area in Raqqa, from the Ghanem al-Zahir clan He said to me, Talal al-Shuweimi You Are you still doing amulet sorcery? Since I’m from the Marandiyya clan, and our ancestors were known for religion and for using drums He said, Do you still make amulet spells and play the drums? And I knew him. I knew him well I told him, I swear, Sheikh I told him these words when you stop playing the rababa [string instrument] He said to me, Are you the brother of Abdul Rahim Shuweimi? I said, Yes, I’m his brother I said, But, Sheikh At that time, my family was trying to use mediations for me My mother, an old woman, wherever she saw a clan sheikh or a member of ISIS Any man with a long beard, she would go to him and say, My son has children, and so on She would follow them around for her son, hoping they’d release me, and I had children My children became my parents’ responsibility, and they had no power to help We argued about this topic Then he said to me, You’re a filthy person I said, So now for you the Muslim is filthy, and Muslims deserve execution It’s the rule of the strong over the weak, and there’s no power or strength He said to me, God willing, you won’t leave this place He brought in a man, and that man Had a medium-sized passenger van His house was in the Kalta area in Raqqa’s countryside, and he used to transport female teachers The group forced teachers to attend repentance courses. The point is, he used to drive those teachers The group accused him of smuggling people out of Raqqa They brought the man and blindfolded him That act was meant as punishment for me I felt it was a punishment for me, and while he was talking to me He made me sit on a chair While I was blindfolded I was only blindfolded and tied with plastic ties The kind used for bundling electric cables Then he brought the man and started beating him with a water hose called “Lakhdar Brahimi” He disfigured the man’s body with the beating The man cried intensely I could hear the hose striking near me, and he kept beating him until he killed him Finally, I was so upset that I said, He’ll kill this man if he continues I told him, I don’t want the interrogation anymore, just stop hitting him, because I realized he was doing it for my sake He couldn’t hit me, not out of fear But because there was no proof against me, so he had no right to beat me And because Abu Yousuf the interrogator and Abu Suhaib the Tunisian were above him in rank Although Abu Suhaib the Tunisian was very strict religiously, he never allowed anyone to be beaten
full_text_by_file.05_المحققين_En.srt: Those who conducted the interrogations with us Were from inside the prison Like Abu Yousuf the interrogator and Abu Abdul Rahman The second one was from the al-Sahou clan The group deliberately used people in interrogations who had strong intelligence connections inside the city That’s one point, and the first and most important The most important and strongest thing they had Was that they had security agents inside the prison They had security agents from our own people, from our towns, sadly ISIS had security agents from our local people working inside the prison for money For example, someone would come, and I’d look at him, and one of them Was from the al-Bou Saraya clan, named Abdullah He sat beside me, and I had been in prison for 10 days When he sat and started crying, saying he was wronged and jailed over selling a pistol I trusted him, as I had nothing to hide The group’s informants or security men Would come acting like victims, saying they were innocent, and talk about ISIS in front of me, saying we’ll get out tomorrow And say, God is generous, and promise that such-and-such faction would enter the city So the person in front of them would confess and start talking These were the ones ISIS sent in as informants and internal agents They were very intelligent people One of these people was named Abdullah al-Asaad His house was next to the Nabhan Glass Shop in Raqqa, and he was killed While fighting in one of the battles He started crying in front of me, and about 40 days later He started crying again and told me that his mother had died When I got out of prison, his goal was to get information from me When I was released, I went to his house to offer condolences to his father And I said, Keep your faith strong, Abdullah is crying and mourning his mother Since I had been released, it was my duty to visit my friend’s family who was still in prison I was shocked when his mother came out, cursing him and saying, May God never grant him success, and that she had disowned him She said, He claims I’m dead, but he’s an informant for the group There were many people who infiltrated others in this same way Our people are kind-hearted and simple So they might slip and say something by mistake, and that security informant Would then Report it to the security forces, and based on that, the person would be accused. Many people were killed that way Stories like this were common
full_text_by_file.06_وسائل التعذيب_En.srt: As for torture methods Torture We witnessed how they tortured people The problem was that they had two styles One underground And another above ground, completely different Like a person with two faces,and both faces were terrifying As for the Hisba (religious police) It had 20 faces Regarding torture methods Anything you can imagine existed. Many locals were imprisoned And subjected to different torture methods,for example, crucifixion Or they’d shackle your hands like this with cuffs When your hands were raised with the cuffs, your ribs would tear apart from here When Abu Seif lifted the prisoner,he was a huge man from eating too much Western food God gave him the strength to eat 4 or 5 sandwiches At the expense of the so-called caliphate He was very strong, while I was weak He would grab me by the neck and lift me about 20 or 30 centimeters off the ground My legs would kick violently, just like in American movies I couldn’t walk or breathe from the pain in my chest I had tonsillitis, my ribs were broken, and to this day I still suffer chest pain At that time The guys, God bless them, took care of me for about 68 days They would take me to the toilets, bathe me, and cared for me well Among them were Ibrahim Abu Nashwa and Ibrahim Ayyoub, known as Shantar All the guys who were with me in prison were kind and helped me in every way I was never cared for in my life the way those six men cared for me when I was the seventh When I was broken and my ribs were shattered in prison
full_text_by_file.07_محاولة الهروب من السجن_En.srt: Out of fear and terror My ribs and legs could barely move In the room above us there were solitary cells, each about one square meter, with a ceiling Barriwi carried me and we went up He broke a small iron piece from one of the solitary cells Then Barriwi started scraping the wall The wall was coated with smooth cement We discovered it was made of modern white stone, not black stone Barriwi said, “Talal al-Shuweimi, let’s escape,” so we tried to run away It was me and Barriwi And Ibrahim Ayyoub, known as Shantar, was watching the door with Bara al-Ano We started digging the wall and dug the length of about two bricks and the width of two more But we were terrified, because each brick Had two sides, one of them thick The thick side was pressed against another thick side, with a cement layer in between that couldn’t be broken easily,it made a loud sound Across from our room, one of the group’s members was standing guard We were terrified I went to the sheikh and told him, Out of fear, no one knows what to do,especially the sentenced ones So I said to the imprisoned sheikh with us My brother Read to us from the Quran, maybe God will save us from them and help us escape Or make them blind to us,things like that, which are close to sorcery, God forbid But we were desperate, and a drowning man clings to a straw As you know, I was sentenced to execution, and a drowning man clings not just to a straw but to the water itself Trying to hold onto the water The sheikh actually believed himself He was a man whose house was on Mansour Street in Raqqa, sentenced to execution On charges of sorcery He said he treated people with traditional Arabic medicine He went to treat a woman who had joint pain in her legs And they arrested him for that, accusing him of witchcraft That man started reciting And it became like some satanic ritual, while we continued pounding on the wall Hard, but the guard outside didn’t hear us The guard sitting outside was an ISIS member who had just finished his training course His name was Abu al-Yaman Abu al-Yaman was from Aleppo Province When we made an opening in the wall, one of us, a diabetic man Named Ibrahim al-Ali al-Alkan, known as Abu Nashwa Got so scared that his blood sugar spiked He almost died And he actually did die They had brought us wool blankets Because winter was approaching We tore up those wool blankets, and also some other covers When we broke through the wall of the Hisba prison, behind it was an investigation building on Sajiyah Street There was a depth of about 7 meters After 7 meters, there was the yard of a ground-floor house Because of the pounding noise, the homeowner came out He shouted, Who are you? I said, Uncle, I’m Talal al-Shuweimi, your relative And Ibrahim al-Fakkash said, I’m from the Fakkash family, from the Shibli Salamah clan We started crying and begged him not to report us,we were sentenced to execution The man cried along with us He started crying and said, My nephews, jump down quickly So they wouldn’t cut off my neck with yours And the man was holding a laser lighter for us He looked at us and said, “My brothers’ sons, hurry up, the daylight has broken,” and when we finished Ibrahim al-Alkan fell ill And the last time we went out The house of Emir Abu Seif was on Sajiyah Street The Hisba (religious police) base was on Sajiyah Street, and the road was like this Sajiyah Street is on the southern side The Hisba is on the northern side, and Abu Seif’s house is on Sajiyah Street The organization had taken that house from a Kurdish family The owner was called Abu Sateef, a well-known bicycle repairman in Raqqa and on Sajiyah Street So Abu Seif took his house Abu Seif, the Emir of the Investigation, took the house of Abu Sateef, the Kurdish bicycle repairman The Hisba base was across from his house, and when he went out He saw that the wall had been destroyed across the width of about two and a half rooms Suddenly, the security men surrounded the area and came up to us They started shooting at my feet and separated me from the other young men Then they brought us back again For the case of crucifixion and suspension, and of course the beatings and insults
full_text_by_file.08_العملية القضائية-_En.srt: I was tortured and kept being tortured for 3 more days After 3 days, they took me to the court There was an air fan in the room where they put us It was installed in the wall for air and odor ventilation, called the sharaq [door vent] I told the guys, “Listen, guys If they take us down to the same room The sharaq switch is below, and we’re placed upstairs in the solitary cells When you turn on the metal sharaq, it will make noise Whoever among you goes down to our old room, turn it on so we know who was sent there” The first one they summoned was al-Bariwi We waited 5 minutes, 15 minutes, half an hour, and the sharaq still didn’t turn on Then they summoned another person too And the sharaq still didn’t turn on Fear and terror spread among us Then they called my name, Talal al-Shuweimi They shackled me and blindfolded me. Abu Seif lifted me by my shoulder and led me And Abu Hudhayfa held me by the other shoulder They carried me forcefully as if they were about to carry out the sentence against me and took me out They said, “Stand,” so I stood He asked me, “Are you so-and-so?” I said, “Yes, I am” He said, “You did this and that.” I said, “Yes, Sheikh, I was the biggest cigarette trader Under the caliphate, but God guided me, and I quit over a year or a year and a half ago” “And now, after I quit the trade, you summon me and treat me like this?” We argued, and the sheikh started insulting me He said, “You’re talkative and loud. You think religion is a game,” and so on He said, “You and that woman,I won’t mention her name, You both smoke hookah and walk around with that other woman” They were all women suffering from poverty and hunger They traded cigarettes to earn a living and support their children Some were widows, others had children, and one had a disabled child They relied on God and borrowed cigarettes from me To sell them, cover their expenses, and provide for their families So I said to him, “Sheikh You’re accusing me of false charges Keep the cigarette charge, it’s better than accusing me of adultery Imagine, I entered prison for cigarette trading and ended up with 10 other charges I had nothing to do with And sadly, I didn’t even know about them until the very end In the end, I learned that the cases I was sentenced for had nothing to do with cigarettes My arrest and interrogation were indeed for cigarette trading But eventually, none of the charges were related to that Even in the same court, they accused me of adultery With one of the women who sold cigarettes Now tell me, does it make sense? I have 3 wives, my daughters are unmarried, and I have 13 children And now I’m going to commit adultery? After 3 wives and after reaching 40 or 45 years old? I told him, “Sheikh, that’s not logical Leave me with the cigarette trading charge, may God guide you Judge me for that,it’s better than judging me for adultery” I told him, “If you don’t fear God, then you should be feared.” He said, “Do you think we don’t fear God?” Then he stood up and hit me. I don’t know with what, because I was blindfolded I felt something running down my head, like heat And that’s when he pronounced the sentence He didn’t give me anything, didn’t even let me sit. He just asked me a few harsh questions Then said, “We sentence you to qisas [retribution] You did such and such” He asked, “What do you think of my sentence?” At that moment, I mocked the judgment I didn’t take it seriously. I was just a cigarette trader,what does qisas have to do with that? Why should I be sentenced to retribution for cigarette trading? They judged me as a corrupter on earth But “corruption on earth” means armed robbery and banditry Armed assault,not cigarette trading And today, the whole world smokes cigarettes Everyone smokes cigarettes Even alcohol existed in the time of the Companions But now, in the time of these “new Companions,” They want to cut off heads and hands But there’s no power or strength left,today, they’re strong and we’re weak He asked me, “What do you say about the judgment?” I said, “Me?”,I wasn’t afraid, I was relaxed I wasn’t scared at all, I was at ease I told him, “I’m satisfied with your judgment.” When I said that, they were surprised He said, “Why are you satisfied?” I said, “Isn’t this God’s decree, the Almighty?” “You’re ruling by God’s sharia, and I’m content with God’s law” Then he said to me “What will you say to the seven-member committee?” I said, “Who are the seven-member committee?” He said, “They’re the sheikhs, because your file will be sent to them” I said, “What difference will my words make?” “You already sentenced me to qisas,what can those sheikhs do now?” He said, “Say whatever you want to tell them” I said, “Send them my regards and tell them the Sheikh’s judgment is false upon falsehood” Your entire verdict is invalid upon invalid My case is about cigarettes, and you accused me of adultery and other charges Just to sentence me to execution How can you sentence me to execution while I’m blindfolded, shackled, and standing on my feet? Then one of them stood up And hit me He punched me in the stomach and I fell to the ground. They held me as another came from behind and hit me Of course, I was blindfolded and didn’t know where the blows were coming from When he hit me, I said, I accept the judgment and law of God Almighty
full_text_by_file.09_الشرطة القضائية_En.srt: When they shackled my left hand, I lifted the cloth blindfold from my eyes I looked and saw that everyone was masked, and at the investigation door There was a black vehicle for transporting prisoners, marked “Judicial Police” When they issued execution sentences They handed the sentenced prisoners to the Judicial Police, which handled executions and amputations At that moment Terror, death, and pain fell upon me It was a situation no one would envy, terrible to the point that It truly defies description, no matter how I try to explain it When I saw the Judicial Police and their black vehicle parked there I saw my friend, Ibrahim al-Fakkash And another man, both shackled They hit me immediately Blindfolded me again, put a black bag over my head, shackled me, and made me sit down I felt that Ibrahim al-Fakkash, out of fear, was repeating, “There is no god but God” I crawled toward him And it was sunset time I asked him, Are you al-Breiwi? He said, Yes. I asked, What did they do? He said, They sentenced me to execution Then we cried,not because we wanted to, but out of sheer terror Our fear wasn’t only of death or being shot But from imagining them holding our heads and slitting our throats with a knife,that’s what truly terrified us Every second we were dying millions of times inside Or a billion times,each time imagining the knife descending on our necks We had already seen ISIS videos showing people being slaughtered with knives Those scenes stuck in my mind, and I imagined the blade cutting through my neck We cried from fear We knew well who ISIS were,they had no mercy, no compassion The third, the fourth, and others came, but one person remained It was during Ramadan, and we were fasting As you know, at sunset, when the call to prayer begins for those fasting Ibrahim al-Alkan said, I want water Before you cut off our heads, we ask you to bring us water,we are fasting So they brought us water We couldn’t see, because we were blindfolded They gave the water to Ibrahim When he drank, the water got stuck in his throat from fear It lodged in his throat, and he began choking I was so terrified, I thought they were strangling us one by one At first, when we heard the sunset call to prayer, we relaxed, thinking no execution would happen then Because they usually wanted crowds to film their productions So when the call to prayer came, we felt some relief, knowing that executions never happened at sunset or night They never carried out executions at that time When Ibrahim started choking, fear overtook me I crawled backward and ended up in the arms of my friend al-Breiwi When I stretched out my right hand, I touched the shoe of one of the ISIS members Each of us had an ISIS member standing right beside him I jumped into al-Breiwi’s arms and told him, They’re going to strangle us one by one Then they brought the last person,I think it was Baraa al-Ano,and shackled him They put us inside the vehicle We could see a little from under the blindfold. Inside the vehicle were sharp metal pieces and swords It felt like we were in a scene from the “Khalid ibn al-Walid” series from the sword era
full_text_by_file.10_وصف سجن مفرق الجزرة_En.srt: They put us in a small isolated room with only a toilet A small room with a toilet recently prepared and designated for us There was a small window in the wall like this It had thick iron bars They brought us new iron chains,thick ones, not thin As if they were meant to restrain some giant beast Like in American or foreign movies There was a huge gorilla in one movie that they enlarged with cameras and computers It felt like they were chaining a giant gorilla with those massive chains The chains were so thick that the prisoner couldn’t even move his hand They shackled us with those chains, and between each person there were 26 links The chains were white and new Abu Seif told me he had bought them especially for me, of the expensive kind He said he bought them exclusively from Qoutli Street They also brought 7 locks They placed the chain on my hand and locked it with a padlock Then left 26 links’ space, chained my friend’s hand, and locked it too And so on The first prisoner was Ibrahim al-Fakkash, known as al-Bariwi And I was second Ibrahim al-Alkan fell ill Then they fasten the chain to the wall Ibrahim al-Fakkash was at the front, and the other end of the chain was fastened to the wall The group cell was reserved for just the seven of us They used to bring us a plate of oil and thyme They would come in wearing masks and bring us food I used to tell Al-Bariwi, our charge isn’t about the cigarette trade They chained us and sentenced us to execution And today this man comes in bringing us food while masked, and we’re bound with iron chains They even spoke to us through cameras and loudspeakers, telling us to move back So we’d retreat and hide in a corner of the room while they brought in a plate of oil and thyme We’d eat like that, and during the first 7 days, we didn’t eat anything until an emir came in He was from the Al-Dabba family, his name was Abu Saqr May God grant him wellness, guide him, and forgive him May God forgive him in this life and the next He came to us and started sitting with us every day for half an hour He would tell us prophetic hadiths May God bless him and guide him And protect him for his children, God willing He began forcing us to eat, like how one feeds a patient in intensive care He’d put a bite of bread in our mouths, forcing us to eat He started easing our suffering a little We stayed like this under Abu Saqr’s care For about three and a half months Around three and a half months, or about three months and 20 days When Abu Saqr’s shift ended, another would take over at night No one dealt with us except Abu Saqr and the plate of thyme I fell ill And the weather turned cold It’s honestly painful to talk about It wasn’t a human act Nor an Islamic one, not even something the Jews of Khaybar would do Because 7 of us sentenced to execution were chained with iron shackles And there were 27 links between each man When I needed to go to the toilets God is witness to what I say When I needed to go to the toilets And I wanted to sit to relieve myself When I sat, my friend next to me had to bend toward me The third had to bend his body, and the fourth had to stand over me Meaning four of us stood in the toilet so one could relieve himself, may God honor you What kind of Islam is that? What sharia allows it? Seven Muslim men chained together, entering the toilets three or four at a time It was disgusting behavior And when one of us got sick Many of the young men got diarrhea, or someone needed to urinate, it was obvious We all had to wake up, because between me and the next man were 26 links, so all 7 of us had to get up Especially when one of us was sick with diarrhea We stayed like that for about three and a half months, or three months and 20 days
full_text_by_file.11_يوميات السجن_En.srt: Our daily routine among ourselves We had no real routine, only reading the Quran, crying, and supplicating Asking God to free us and the nation of Muhammad At night, we’d sit Reciting the Quran and praying Fajr until sunrise We’d fall asleep drowsy, still in our chains We barely slept half an hour or less before waking up in fear,some crying, some deep in thought Some would sit alone in the corner, imagining death When they were about to execute one or a group, the organization would film an ISIS video On a disc or flash drive Abu Seif brought us a flash drive back then, and we watched it It was like horror movies, we were sentenced to death, and he made us watch people being beheaded May God not grant him success in this world or the next He showed us footage of an execution slaughterhouse in Deir ez-Zor Province Where they executed 35 people hanging from sharp iron hooks One emir was carrying two men like this, wearing a short white robe,he was from Deir ez-Zor He carried two men like that He killed them and hung them on iron hooks Then Mustafa, known as Abu Seif Said to me, “Look, Talal al-Shuweimi, do you see the execution prep, Talal al-Shuweimi?”
full_text_by_file.12_العناية الطبية_En.srt: Sometimes the hallucinations and fear cause sicknesses like diarrhea, chills, and psychological illness There was a man with us named Qadri al-Dabba Qadri al-Dabba started screaming in his sleep His eyes bulged, foam came out of his mouth,he became seriously ill, not normal at all Interviewer: Did the organization help him, or did they leave him to suffer? They didn’t give him anything, his fits would come every 4 or 5 hours One time, I fell sick myself I got chills and severe diarrhea inside the prison It knocked me to the ground, and I was near death By God’s power, I recovered My friends spoke to the ISIS members, but they didn’t give me injections or any help That happened during the days I was sentenced to death I don’t know if my illness came from fear or was a real sickness When I fell ill, there was a jailer under Abu Seif named Abu Abdul Malik He was from Tabqa, originally from Aleppo Province He watched us through the cameras, even Ibrahim al-Fakkash started crying over me And Ibrahim al-Alkan recited Quran over me, rubbing my body and weeping, thinking I was dying And Ibrahim al-Ayyoub, nicknamed Shantar, was a simple, impatient man He stood up, started smashing the door, shouting, “Talal al-Shuweimi is dead!” Abu Abdul Malik answered him And said, when he dies When he dies, let us know so we can take him out,that’s what Abu Abdul Malik said Praise be to God, Talal al-Shuweimi is still alive by God’s grace Abu Abdul Malik was later struck by an airstrike on his way to Tabqa May God have mercy on him,I recited Al-Fatiha for him
full_text_by_file.13_وساطات_En.srt: There were mediations everywhere But not before God Almighty,there’s no mediation, and no bribery works When I was sentenced to death, neither my mother, my father, nor anyone could help me Only God Almighty can help you And your deeds help you in their eyes But they still had mediations One of my neighbors was detained His name was Qusai Ahmad al-Omara,he paid them 5,000 dollars and was released In the final days, for example I was under the death sentence when the judicial committee arrived They started bringing me my wife Then they started bringing me my second wife Of course, in exchange for money, whoever wanted the price of a motorcycle, I gave it to him And whoever wanted diesel for his house, I gave it to him There were many things I benefited from after my family found out that I was in prison We started playing openly When my family found out that I was in prison My wives started And even Almost every day they came to visit me, one day they came, one day they didn’t Of course secretly, so no one would see us outside The prison was in a narrow corner facing my family’s house, and I saw them secretly They brought silver and gold coins, and their food was now paid for by us personally We were 7 people sentenced to execution Every day one of us paid for the food, the seven of us, and this prison It seemed that the jailers, the emir, and the rest of the group bringing food were all from poor families When they brought food, they gave it to the other prisoners Because the prison had two sections: one for the rest of the prisoners and one just for us seven We seven took turns. For example, if it was my turn, my wife knew that today was my turn She brought food for me and for the guards, like fried chicken one day and grilled kebab another She brought us ready meals from the market, and there was plenty. My turn came once a week since we were 7 people Every day food was paid for by one of us, so my turn came once a week to bring the food The food bill was about 75 to 100 thousand per month, all ready food from the market for us And the ISIS members started sitting and spending evenings with us They chatted with us, and we bought nuts and soft drinks, though we were shackled They trusted us and learned our story They told us, “Your charge is collaboration with the Global Coalition, and you’re sentenced to execution” That actually helped us a lot in planning our escape
full_text_by_file.14_قطعة منشار مقابل عشرة ملايين_En.srt: But once, we had a jailer who wasn’t just a jailer,he was also an emir in ISIS In the Mafarq al-Jazra area They said he was one of the “Kanas” family. I remember only his nickname, I forgot his name, but he was a good man too In the morning, the emir Abu Saqr from the al-Dabba family came He was on morning duty as the emir At night, the shift changed. That man left around sunset, and another took over He was from Mafarq al-Jazra and was well known He told me he was married to two women Sometimes he came and sat with us, but ISIS put him under surveillance because he was kind and sympathetic to prisoners We paid him 7 million to bring us just a piece of a saw So we could escape, we told him We’re 7 prisoners. If you can just bring us a small metal saw blade Just so we could cut the big lock hanging on the wall To escape We told him each of us would give you one million. He said, “ISIS will kill me.” We said, “You’ll escape with us” Praise God, he was supposed to bring us the saw blade But the next day, they arrested him They transferred him. We didn’t know whether he was detained or relocated, but we never saw him again after that
full_text_by_file.15_المساومة_En.srt: When they tried to bargain with me They bargained with me a lot, even the well-known emirs There were prominent figures among them Specifically, one from the al-Amer family and one from the al-Mukhtar family bargained with me They told me, “Bring us fertilizer” Fertilizer 33, the one they use to make explosives They bargained with me to bring cigarettes and hide the fertilizer inside them for them Many people bargained with me, not just them Even Abu Seif himself bargained with me to work with him And to pass on reports and information to him He told me, “Whatever you want in return, you’ll get it” I told him, “I’m not that kind of man”
full_text_by_file.16_تفاصيل في الذاكرة_En.srt: One day, the organization took me Abu Seif came to me. The first time, he came with Abu Khaled al-Idlibi and other men They took me away They said, “Come with us to carry out your sentence” Of course They took me along with six other men Among them was a man named Abdul Karim al-Ghaffaf He was from the al-Marandi clan He was one of my uncles, and his charge was adopting Shia beliefs An old man, about 80 years old There was also another man from the al-Bu Assaf clan named Yasin al-Yasin Anyway There were six men, and I was the seventh They took me in a car, and I felt that we had left the city They made us get out, and there was a cool breeze in the air They made us kneel on our knees And when they made us kneel One of the emirs said, “Remove the cloth blindfold from Talal al-Shuweimi’s eyes” They removed the blindfold from my eyes Then I looked to my right and left and saw that everyone was lined up on both sides They told them, “You did this and that, say the two testimonies” So they recited the testimonies, and each one was shot in the head I looked around and the place was full of trees,it used to be a Baath Pioneers camp When we were kids, we used to go there in summer for school camps during the Assad regime One of them said to me, “Talal al-Shuweimi” What do you want? I said, I don’t want anything. You brought me here and sentenced me to execution He said to me They removed the cloth blindfold from my eyes so I could see around me Among them were well-known people,some of the senior figures in the ISIS state High-ranking figures who are still alive today He said to me Now we’ll carry out the sentence and get rid of you Then another masked man came And said to me What is your last wish? At that moment I told him, to speak with my children He placed the pistol on my head He said, “Say the two testimonies,” so I did Then he struck me As if he hit me in the head, but he fired the shot beside my ear When he fired beside my ear, out of terror and horror My hands were tied behind my back, and I fell to the ground From the terror of it,it was like an American movie I mean, something Something indescribable, something even the Jews wouldn’t envy you for Nor the Christians, nor Israel, nor even black devils would wish to see you in such a position They wouldn’t even gloat over you
full_text_by_file.17_الخروج_En.srt: The last time I was imprisoned,as I said earlier,they transferred me from one prison to another To a prison hidden in a corner Sadly, that prison was across from my mother’s house My mother’s name is Zahia And one of the emirs, named Muhammad al-Abadi Used to tell me, “Zahia’s son, I saw Zahia walking in the garden” We were on the second floor, and he said, “I saw your mother” The man was joking with me We escaped from the prison during the days of the Euphrates Dam collapse in Raqqa There were several of us, and that same man was with us His name was Abu Muhammad al-Abadi May God bless and reward him We made a hole in the prison wall He escaped with us, helped us, and watched the road. There were seven of us, and a car was waiting We escaped by car, the seven of us
full_text_by_file.18_تجارة السجائر_En.srt: As for me, there’s a saying: they asked the nail, “Why did you go into the wall?” He answered, “From how much they hit my head” I didn’t sell cigarettes by choice, but I was an employee My salary was 12,000 Syrian Lira, working in the Water Authority After I started working, Air Force Intelligence raided my house and detained my brother, taking him to Sednaya Prison When they raided my place and I fled, I had 10 children They needed food and bread I bought a taxi without papers and drove it, but the income wasn’t enough And cigarette trade wasn’t punishable by execution or anything like that But the organization imposed execution on us, even though it wasn’t for cigarette trade Their judgments became vindictive They invented charges,collaboration, cigarettes, ammunition,just to sentence us to execution I was forced into cigarette trade. I have children and pay rent for two houses Every day I’d buy bread for 2,000 or 3,000 Syrian Lira, and there was no other work With the organization, either you join them or remain jobless So how could I feed my children? Should I trade cigarettes to earn a living and feed them, or let them starve to death? Just like many others, there were about 2,000 people And I was under a death sentence
full_text_by_file.19_أثرالسجن_En.srt: Prison had a huge impact on me When I first escaped from prison I started crying Tears of joy, not sadness I began kissing the ground I looked up to God Almighty, crying and shouting, breathing in the air So many things happened to me Prison affected me deeply,like forgetfulness, for example It left me with memory loss Tragic events that scarred me In my life, I never held hatred toward anyone But they made my heart hard They killed the mercy inside me as a human being belonging to humanity They ignored so many rights,human rights, and my rights as a Muslim As a father and provider for a family, for example They stripped the mercy from within me There are still many things inside me ISIS affected me deeply, and I still suffer from what’s called forgetfulness On top of that, the greatest hardship remains,that only God provides and sustains us God Almighty is the Provider For me, ISIS destroyed my life Why am I still struggling today to buy bread? Why am I looking for a job as a taxi driver for daily pay? What’s the reason? Because of an ignorant man Because of an infidel Who should be in prison for two or three years God willing, may the Almighty take from my life And give it to his Just so I live long enough until he gets out of prison, to see him even for one hour To settle my score with him An eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth, and the aggressor is the unjust one Everyone dies once Better that than dying every hour before your children
full_text_by_file.طلال الشويمي-English.docx: 1 00:00:10,219 --> 00:00:12,382 My name is Talal Mustafa al-Shuweimi 2 00:00:12,555 --> 00:00:15,710 I’m from Raqqa Province, living on Sajiyah Street 3 00:00:17,570 --> 00:00:19,476 I’m 45 years old 4 00:00:21,703 --> 00:00:25,906 I have 3 wives and 13 children 5 00:00:29,679 --> 00:00:34,405 During the Assad regime’s control of Raqqa, I worked at the Water Department 6 00:00:35,336 --> 00:00:37,901 After the problems and battles started 7 00:00:39,699 --> 00:00:41,375 I was dismissed from work 8 00:00:42,789 --> 00:00:45,307 The regime accused me of being a terrorist 9 00:00:45,820 --> 00:00:48,437 The Air Force Intelligence raided my house 10 00:00:49,070 --> 00:00:50,461 Because they claimed I was a terrorist 11 00:00:50,902 --> 00:00:52,469 The dismissal order came from Damascus 12 00:00:52,477 --> 00:00:53,477 Of course 13 00:00:53,765 --> 00:00:55,807 It was all a malicious report 14 00:00:56,929 --> 00:00:59,359 Because we were locals and stayed in the city 15 00:01:00,889 --> 00:01:04,115 My dismissal order came in 2012 16 00:01:04,906 --> 00:01:07,599 So I fled Raqqa for a while 17 00:01:07,719 --> 00:01:08,844 And my brother was arrested 18 00:01:09,547 --> 00:01:10,797 By the Assad regime 19 00:01:11,679 --> 00:01:12,922 On behalf of the regime 20 00:01:13,258 --> 00:01:17,609 They put him in Sednaya Prison for about 6 years 21 00:01:19,929 --> 00:01:24,804 After they detained my brother for 6 years in prison 22 00:01:27,108 --> 00:01:29,148 The Free Syrian Army entered Raqqa 23 00:01:29,172 --> 00:01:31,773 I was already dismissed from work as a “terrorist” 24 00:01:32,219 --> 00:01:35,039 About 6 or 7 months after the FSA entered 25 00:01:35,445 --> 00:01:40,126 ISIS entered the city, calling itself the Islamic State 26 00:01:41,179 --> 00:01:43,617 They took control of the area 27 00:01:44,351 --> 00:01:47,648 Within their first 2 or 3 months of settling in 28 00:01:48,344 --> 00:01:53,101 They arrested me, accusing me of being a regime agent—a shabiha [pro-regime thug] 29 00:01:54,758 --> 00:02:01,031 They detained me at Point 11, with the security members 30 00:02:01,469 --> 00:02:03,402 Inside the former Governor’s Palace 31 00:02:03,672 --> 00:02:05,707 I stayed there for about a week 32 00:02:06,794 --> 00:02:08,664 After the Governor’s Palace, they took me 33 00:02:09,648 --> 00:02:14,414 To a new security base near the National Hospital area in Raqqa 34 00:02:15,200 --> 00:02:17,606 It was inside the old Military Judiciary building of the Assad regime 35 00:02:19,289 --> 00:02:23,113 A week later, they moved me from the Governor’s Palace basement 36 00:02:23,893 --> 00:02:27,324 To the old Military Judiciary building near the Traffic Circle 37 00:02:27,349 --> 00:02:28,511 I stayed in that base 38 00:02:30,037 --> 00:02:32,112 Under the same charge, regime agent 39 00:02:32,474 --> 00:02:34,519 I stayed there for 53 days 40 00:02:34,646 --> 00:02:36,240 After 53 days 41 00:02:37,402 --> 00:02:40,409 They released me because there was no evidence against me 42 00:02:40,654 --> 00:02:43,912 The one who interrogated me 43 00:02:44,763 --> 00:02:47,404 Was from Raqqa Province, from Tabqa city 44 00:02:48,308 --> 00:02:49,612 His family name was Khameesi 45 00:02:50,459 --> 00:02:52,019 He was called Abu Yousuf, the interrogator 46 00:02:52,146 --> 00:02:53,456 He was the head of interrogators 47 00:02:54,693 --> 00:02:57,518 Another man was nicknamed Abu Abdurrahman 48 00:02:58,865 --> 00:03:01,979 His name was Mahmoud al-Ali al-Sahou from the Ghanem al-Zahir clan 49 00:03:02,224 --> 00:03:03,815 From an area called al-Jaif 50 00:03:04,217 --> 00:03:07,224 East of the al-Eywij area 51 00:03:07,795 --> 00:03:09,604 Near Rashid Farm in Raqqa 52 00:03:11,310 --> 00:03:15,146 I stayed under interrogation for about 53 days 53 00:03:15,171 --> 00:03:19,053 After they confirmed there was nothing against me 54 00:03:19,162 --> 00:03:22,576 They realized I’d already been dismissed by the Assad regime as a “terrorist” 55 00:03:22,901 --> 00:03:24,197 So they released me 56 00:03:24,623 --> 00:03:26,916 And gave me a clearance card 57 00:03:28,224 --> 00:03:30,379 A certificate proving there were no charges against me 58 00:03:30,404 --> 00:03:34,431 As a Syrian citizen 59 00:03:34,717 --> 00:03:35,722 And a Sunni by faith 60 00:03:35,747 --> 00:03:38,989 I went back to work with the other workers 61 00:03:39,888 --> 00:03:42,521 Trying to earn a living to support my children 62 00:03:44,482 --> 00:03:47,403 Around 50 or 60 days after returning 63 00:03:48,138 --> 00:03:49,248 I was surprised to see someone 64 00:03:49,740 --> 00:03:55,303 Sadly, he was from the same neighborhood, Sunni, and a local of the area 65 00:03:56,287 --> 00:03:57,902 His name was Mahmoud al-Shahada 66 00:03:58,974 --> 00:04:00,769 He was an ISIS emir overseeing the Water Department 67 00:04:00,794 --> 00:04:02,107 His name was Mahmoud al-Shahada 68 00:04:02,404 --> 00:04:03,810 Nicknamed Abu Hudhayfa 69 00:04:03,896 --> 00:04:07,795 Abu Hudhayfa had been detained in Assad regime prisons back in 2000 70 00:04:07,834 --> 00:04:09,209 Accused of joining Jund al-Sham 71 00:04:10,271 --> 00:04:14,193 He struck a deal with the regime in Sednaya Prison and was released 72 00:04:14,303 --> 00:04:18,201 Then brought to Raqqa and appointed as an emir in the caliphate 73 00:04:18,898 --> 00:04:24,935 Mahmoud al-Shahada, known as Abu Hudhayfa, and his brother were both active and skilled 74 00:04:25,217 --> 00:04:26,732 His brother’s name was Hassan 75 00:04:27,123 --> 00:04:28,934 He had two brothers, Hassan and Hassaan 76 00:04:28,959 --> 00:04:31,719 His brother was an emir and his right-hand man 77 00:04:32,404 --> 00:04:34,600 When I came back to the Water Department 78 00:04:35,696 --> 00:04:39,818 I was surprised he’d become the department’s emir after being released from Sednaya 79 00:04:40,037 --> 00:04:43,443 When the workers learned I was coming to work again 80 00:04:43,468 --> 00:04:47,998 I had no salary, but people gave us charity for the children 81 00:04:48,199 --> 00:04:51,364 They offered us odd jobs outside 82 00:04:51,389 --> 00:04:55,303 Of course, we had no fixed income since I and others were dismissed from work 83 00:04:56,357 --> 00:04:58,639 Then Abu Hudhayfa came 84 00:05:00,212 --> 00:05:02,906 And arrested me himself—Mahmoud al-Shahada 85 00:05:03,703 --> 00:05:08,006 By then, he’d become “Minister of the Caliphate” because of his influence and knowledge 86 00:05:08,537 --> 00:05:11,120 He had many initiatives, so he arrested me 87 00:05:11,443 --> 00:05:13,808 He tricked me into it 88 00:05:14,959 --> 00:05:17,404 At the time, we were fixing a broken water line 89 00:05:17,474 --> 00:05:20,206 In the Khasra Faraj area, and when we came 90 00:05:21,089 --> 00:05:24,584 He said, “The sheikh wants to see you” 91 00:05:24,608 --> 00:05:29,959 “He wants to ask a few questions, and there’s also a broken pipe at the security point” 92 00:05:30,467 --> 00:05:33,998 He said, “You should fix it.” I said, “As you wish, Abu Hudhayfa” 93 00:05:34,495 --> 00:05:41,615 When I arrived there, I only saw weapons pointed at me 94 00:05:41,685 --> 00:05:45,123 They threw me to the ground, stepped on my neck, and arrested me 95 00:05:45,494 --> 00:05:47,025 For about two months 96 00:05:47,799 --> 00:05:50,006 When I returned to work at the Water Department 97 00:05:50,670 --> 00:05:52,607 Abu Hudhayfa grabbed me by the hair 98 00:05:52,990 --> 00:05:54,334 Dragged me toward the table 99 00:05:54,670 --> 00:05:56,217 Pressed my head onto it 100 00:05:56,498 --> 00:05:59,381 And placed his foot on my left shoulder 101 00:05:59,967 --> 00:06:04,084 He said to me, “You’re a shabih (pro-regime thug). You shouldn’t enter the institution. You’re an agent for the regime” 102 00:06:04,560 --> 00:06:09,602 I told him, Calm down, Sheikh, this is my clearance from Point 11, and I’m clean, there’s nothing against me 103 00:06:09,927 --> 00:06:12,170 I have children, but he said, No, you’re a shabih 104 00:06:12,740 --> 00:06:14,896 And no one will be able to get you back to work 105 00:06:16,188 --> 00:06:20,719 We brought mediations from one sheikh and another, but nothing worked 106 00:06:21,501 --> 00:06:23,842 At that point, I had a small amount of money 107 00:06:24,052 --> 00:06:26,373 And my brother had about 125,000 Syrian pounds 108 00:06:26,701 --> 00:06:28,248 So I bought a taxi 109 00:06:28,935 --> 00:06:30,459 A taxi with no official papers 110 00:06:30,484 --> 00:06:33,229 Back then they used to call it “Arouriya” 111 00:06:33,254 --> 00:06:37,435 Because it was stolen—they’d steal and sell it without papers 112 00:06:37,804 --> 00:06:39,404 So they called it an “Arouriya car” 113 00:06:40,638 --> 00:06:43,654 I worked as a taxi driver 114 00:06:44,427 --> 00:06:46,523 For about 5 to 6 months 115 00:06:47,492 --> 00:06:49,326 One day 116 00:06:51,873 --> 00:06:54,170 The Hisba (religious police) raided me at my home 117 00:06:56,248 --> 00:06:57,693 On 16th Street 118 00:06:59,000 --> 00:07:03,101 They said to me, You’re a man who trades cigarettes 119 00:07:03,724 --> 00:07:05,818 And they detained me for 4 days 120 00:07:07,763 --> 00:07:13,101 They insulted me, but it wasn’t severe or extreme 121 00:07:13,443 --> 00:07:15,719 They kept saying, You’re a cigarette dealer, and so on 122 00:07:16,021 --> 00:07:21,568 I told him, Sheikh, I’m not a cigarette dealer, I don’t even smoke cigarettes, but yes, I smoke hookah 123 00:07:22,373 --> 00:07:23,709 Yes, I smoke hookah 124 00:07:24,521 --> 00:07:28,206 But I don’t smoke cigarettes. If I did, I’d tell you 125 00:07:28,810 --> 00:07:30,193 Anyway, they detained me 126 00:07:30,865 --> 00:07:31,865 One man 127 00:07:31,920 --> 00:07:35,302 And took me to a Hisba base called “Panorama” 128 00:07:36,201 --> 00:07:39,310 It’s in Raqqa, near Maqs Roundabout, called “Panorama” 129 00:07:39,724 --> 00:07:42,520 There was a Hisba base there. The emir in charge was Abu Bishr 130 00:07:42,545 --> 00:07:44,146 Abu Bishr, a Saudi national 131 00:07:45,388 --> 00:07:47,208 After 4 days of detention 132 00:07:49,091 --> 00:07:53,269 I moved my home to another neighborhood, fearing someone was targeting me 133 00:07:53,294 --> 00:07:57,810 I was hungry, and I had 12 or 13 children 134 00:07:59,145 --> 00:08:00,364 There was no strength left in me 135 00:08:02,310 --> 00:08:06,106 I thought, There are people who trade cigarettes and make a living from it 136 00:08:06,856 --> 00:08:10,786 So I started selling cigarettes. Honestly, the accusation already stuck to me anyway 137 00:08:11,200 --> 00:08:14,067 So I decided to trade cigarettes 138 00:08:14,981 --> 00:08:16,356 And I began working in that trade 139 00:08:17,060 --> 00:08:19,123 For about a month or a month and a half 140 00:08:20,052 --> 00:08:23,005 Then a Hisba patrol came and arrested me 141 00:08:23,669 --> 00:08:26,450 They detained me for about 15 days 142 00:08:27,645 --> 00:08:28,912 And the one who arrested me 143 00:08:29,942 --> 00:08:35,528 Was a man from Rumaila named Mustafa al-Hamid al-Muslih 144 00:08:36,341 --> 00:08:37,934 Known as Abu Seif Maqs 145 00:08:38,583 --> 00:08:41,333 He also had another nom de guerre, Abu Seif Doshka 146 00:08:42,026 --> 00:08:43,799 Also known as Abu Seif Maqs 147 00:08:43,958 --> 00:08:47,489 He was originally from the Wahb clan 148 00:08:47,880 --> 00:08:51,302 His maternal uncles were from the Masaar group of the Barij clan 149 00:08:52,216 --> 00:08:54,591 As for Abu Seif Maqs, his past 150 00:08:56,450 --> 00:08:58,018 Was extremely bad 151 00:08:58,747 --> 00:09:02,114 He used to take hashish and pills and was unemployed 152 00:09:02,864 --> 00:09:05,560 And strangely enough 153 00:09:06,224 --> 00:09:09,877 When ISIS entered, I was shocked to see he had become a Doshka gunner 154 00:09:09,893 --> 00:09:13,036 Then, since he was from the area and knew people 155 00:09:13,528 --> 00:09:16,911 They made him emir of the Search and Investigation Unit, this man called Mustafa 156 00:09:17,919 --> 00:09:19,692 Mustafa arrested me 157 00:09:20,341 --> 00:09:24,630 For the second time, for about 15 days. He tortured, beat, and insulted me 158 00:09:25,122 --> 00:09:26,802 Of course, they found no cigarettes with me 159 00:09:28,239 --> 00:09:32,849 The emir of the Search Unit was the same Mustafa al-Muslih 160 00:09:33,638 --> 00:09:34,828 Known as Abu Seif 161 00:09:34,950 --> 00:09:39,724 And another man called Ibrahim al-Ali al-Khayheet 162 00:09:40,466 --> 00:09:44,515 From the Ghanem al-Zahir clan, living near the Jazra Junction in Raqqa 163 00:09:46,052 --> 00:09:47,802 Known as Abu Hudhayfa 164 00:09:48,669 --> 00:09:51,005 Abu Hudhayfa was about 30 years old 165 00:09:51,575 --> 00:09:55,145 The sharia official was Abu Hamza al-Tunsi 166 00:09:56,435 --> 00:09:59,406 There was another man, originally from Sukhna, living on Sajiyah Street in Raqqa 167 00:09:59,685 --> 00:10:00,716 Named Abu Zayd 168 00:10:01,450 --> 00:10:03,841 His brother was the emir of the Islamic Police 169 00:10:04,023 --> 00:10:06,843 In Raqqa Province, overseeing the whole wilaya 170 00:10:07,083 --> 00:10:08,786 Abu Zayd was a sharia official 171 00:10:08,844 --> 00:10:12,125 Second in rank under Abu Hamza al-Tunsi 172 00:10:12,226 --> 00:10:18,044 He worked as a messenger between the court and the Hisba’s sharia office 173 00:10:19,286 --> 00:10:21,645 As for how many times I was imprisoned 174 00:10:21,670 --> 00:10:27,505 If I tried to describe my suffering, I’d need 3 months to finish 175 00:10:27,810 --> 00:10:31,231 The torture and misery we endured 176 00:10:31,661 --> 00:10:34,935 I was imprisoned 28 times 177 00:10:34,960 --> 00:10:38,890 Some people might think I’m lying, but the locals know me well 178 00:10:39,052 --> 00:10:43,296 My bag was always ready, because every 2 or 3 days ISIS would arrest me 179 00:10:43,740 --> 00:10:47,515 The first time I was jailed in a security checkpoint, the charge was suspicion 180 00:10:47,763 --> 00:10:50,255 I was imprisoned there for 56 days 181 00:10:50,786 --> 00:10:55,802 The other 27 times were all for cigarette trading 182 00:10:56,794 --> 00:11:00,872 My detentions ranged between 10 to 15 days, sometimes up to 2 months 183 00:11:01,028 --> 00:11:05,996 Then they’d release me, since they found no evidence, and I was very cautious around them 184 00:11:06,544 --> 00:11:08,052 The last time 185 00:11:09,443 --> 00:11:10,443 They 186 00:11:11,325 --> 00:11:15,325 Launched a campaign against cigarette sellers, and ISIS members specifically targeted me 187 00:11:15,567 --> 00:11:19,512 Me and 2 or 3 other people 188 00:11:19,739 --> 00:11:22,403 7 people, including me, were sentenced to qisas [execution by retribution] 189 00:11:22,898 --> 00:11:24,410 They sentenced them to qisas with me too 190 00:11:26,590 --> 00:11:28,778 They summoned me and took me by deceit 191 00:11:30,114 --> 00:11:31,692 They tricked me and took me away 192 00:11:33,723 --> 00:11:38,112 They called me on the phone and said, Are you Talal al-Shuweimi? I said, Yes, I am Talal al-Shuweimi 193 00:11:38,137 --> 00:11:40,145 They said, Someone confessed against you 194 00:11:40,762 --> 00:11:43,004 He said you sold him 2 cartons of cigarettes 195 00:11:44,285 --> 00:11:46,746 That wasn’t true at all 196 00:11:46,771 --> 00:11:49,895 Because I hadn’t personally sold cigarettes for about a year 197 00:11:50,316 --> 00:11:52,731 Yes, I was a cigarette dealer 198 00:11:54,004 --> 00:11:55,004 But 199 00:11:55,285 --> 00:11:58,417 When I was in the trade, I didn’t distribute or sell cigarettes myself 200 00:11:59,340 --> 00:12:01,028 I didn’t distribute or sell them 201 00:12:02,004 --> 00:12:04,410 When they summoned me, I told them, I’ll come right away 202 00:12:04,738 --> 00:12:09,496 I got in my car and went to the Hisba, which was at the end of Sajiyah Street 203 00:12:10,279 --> 00:12:12,464 On Atiya Bakery Street 204 00:12:13,303 --> 00:12:15,644 And the Hisba emir who was on Sajiyah Street 205 00:12:15,761 --> 00:12:19,738 Was named Issa, from the Jais clan, known as Abu Humam 206 00:12:20,566 --> 00:12:22,207 And Abu Humam, before that 207 00:12:22,527 --> 00:12:27,246 Had been a sergeant in the Political Security branch under the Assad regime 208 00:12:28,809 --> 00:12:32,504 When he was a sergeant under the Assad regime and ISIS was established 209 00:12:33,129 --> 00:12:38,262 Abu Humam came and announced his defection and joined the Islamic State 210 00:12:41,603 --> 00:12:45,814 When Abu Humam summoned me, he had already raided my house about 10 times 211 00:12:46,231 --> 00:12:51,010 Out of 27 raids, he came several times, knowing they’d never find cigarettes with me 212 00:12:51,331 --> 00:12:55,209 One time he knocked on my door and called out, “Hey Shuweimi!” 213 00:12:55,457 --> 00:12:59,099 Your hookah is running, and we know you don’t have cigarettes, but bring the hookah and come 214 00:12:59,124 --> 00:13:02,317 So I carried the hookah with its coals and placed it in his car 215 00:13:03,215 --> 00:13:05,606 When Abu Humam summoned me, I asked him, Where’s the man? 216 00:13:07,319 --> 00:13:10,819 Who testified against me? He told me, Rest for 5 minutes 217 00:13:12,385 --> 00:13:14,301 Now I’ll call the man 218 00:13:14,981 --> 00:13:17,588 When he called me, he said the man is here with me, come see him 219 00:13:17,613 --> 00:13:19,665 When I went, it turned out there was no man there at all 220 00:13:19,690 --> 00:13:21,621 He said, In 5 minutes he’ll come 221 00:13:21,994 --> 00:13:23,400 So I waited 5 minutes 222 00:13:23,566 --> 00:13:26,209 And said, Abu Humam, where’s the man? He told me, You sold cigarettes 223 00:13:26,226 --> 00:13:27,231 I said, Sheikh 224 00:13:28,051 --> 00:13:31,616 I swore to him by God, and honestly, I swear truthfully, I don’t sell cigarettes 225 00:13:31,973 --> 00:13:35,704 And I don’t distribute cigarettes. I have workers who sell and deliver instead of me 226 00:13:36,777 --> 00:13:38,910 What surprised me was his attitude, and I sat down 227 00:13:39,527 --> 00:13:44,410 I told him, Abu Humam, let me tell you something. Even if I sold two boxes of cigarettes 228 00:13:44,738 --> 00:13:48,191 You’re not smarter than Abu Seif. If he were in your place, he would’ve arrested me right away 229 00:13:49,011 --> 00:13:51,894 The news would’ve reached Abu Seif before it reached you 230 00:13:53,745 --> 00:13:56,660 He said, God willing, and started laughing 231 00:13:57,988 --> 00:14:01,345 Of course he’s ignorant and doesn’t understand religion, and so is Abu Seif 232 00:14:01,370 --> 00:14:05,128 When I quote hadiths to him, he tells me, Fear God for yourself 233 00:14:05,511 --> 00:14:07,788 Even the phrase “Fear God for yourself” 234 00:14:08,199 --> 00:14:12,167 He doesn’t even know what it means. Imagine, when I quoted a hadith to him 235 00:14:12,297 --> 00:14:15,718 He said, Fear God for yourself — meaning he’s ignorant 236 00:14:16,316 --> 00:14:17,877 The word ignorant means lack of knowledge 237 00:14:17,910 --> 00:14:19,314 And Islam is as vast as the sea 238 00:14:19,339 --> 00:14:22,706 Whatever you learn in Islam today is just a drop in that sea 239 00:14:24,175 --> 00:14:26,472 I sat there and was shocked when 240 00:14:26,855 --> 00:14:30,011 Mustafa, known as Abu Seif Maqs, the Emir of the Investigation Unit, entered 241 00:14:30,895 --> 00:14:33,996 And Ibrahim al-Ali al-Khayheet, known as Abu Hudhayfa 242 00:14:34,192 --> 00:14:36,628 Both masked, along with 4 or 5 others 243 00:14:37,050 --> 00:14:39,605 They blindfolded me and tied my hands 244 00:14:40,269 --> 00:14:42,113 And took me to the Investigation Section 245 00:14:42,316 --> 00:14:46,566 Along with 3 or 4 masked members as well 246 00:14:46,785 --> 00:14:48,761 They put me in a
