small Kia Rio, Korean-made 247 00:14:48,786 --> 00:14:55,603 I knew all their cars and the license plates they used during and after work hours 248 00:14:55,683 --> 00:14:57,167 They put me in the Kia Rio 249 00:14:57,620 --> 00:15:02,520 The moment Abu Seif grabbed me — my beard was long, almost reaching my navel 250 00:15:02,902 --> 00:15:06,535 I hadn’t shaved my beard since 2011 251 00:15:07,956 --> 00:15:11,423 He grabbed my beard violently and started banging my head against the wall 252 00:15:11,535 --> 00:15:14,501 His fingernails dug into my face and blood began to come out 253 00:15:14,683 --> 00:15:16,415 Blood was running from my face 254 00:15:16,900 --> 00:15:19,636 I didn’t feel it at first, but I felt warmth 255 00:15:19,878 --> 00:15:23,696 While blindfolded, I put my hand on my face and found real blood 256 00:15:24,408 --> 00:15:26,972 They took me to the Investigation Section and up to the second floor 257 00:15:29,831 --> 00:15:34,527 They brought me into a room on the second floor, where the Investigation Section was located 258 00:15:35,118 --> 00:15:39,965 It was headed by Abu Hamza al-Tunisi, the senior sharia official and overall emir 259 00:15:39,990 --> 00:15:43,122 Under him were the Emir of Investigation, Abu Seif Maqs, and Abu Hudhayfa 260 00:15:43,676 --> 00:15:46,418 And Abu Zaid, from the Sakhani clan 261 00:15:46,896 --> 00:15:49,549 His brother, Fayez, was the Emir of the Islamic Police 262 00:15:49,574 --> 00:15:51,012 They took me upstairs 263 00:15:51,919 --> 00:15:53,787 My hands were tied behind my back 264 00:15:54,380 --> 00:15:56,987 And I was blindfolded, and they began beating me 265 00:15:57,012 --> 00:16:01,004 They hit me with sticks and metal rods — the beating was extremely painful 266 00:16:02,185 --> 00:16:06,177 I was beaten for 3 days straight 267 00:16:06,985 --> 00:16:10,677 They poured water on me and kept beating me again and again 268 00:16:11,123 --> 00:16:16,110 When I woke up, I found myself hanging on the wall on the second floor 269 00:16:16,498 --> 00:16:22,497 That area was at the start of Sajiyah Street, at the intersection with Hittin School Street 270 00:16:24,107 --> 00:16:26,497 That place was known as the Investigation Zone 271 00:16:26,584 --> 00:16:27,998 I found myself hanging 272 00:16:28,779 --> 00:16:31,497 I remained hanging on the wall for 18 days 273 00:16:33,013 --> 00:16:36,404 It was the middle of the hot summer season 274 00:16:36,927 --> 00:16:40,326 But our Lord, praise be to Him, is merciful in His mercy 275 00:16:40,701 --> 00:16:42,216 And in His glory and majesty 276 00:16:42,685 --> 00:16:45,623 The heat actually helped me endure the beatings and pain 277 00:16:45,648 --> 00:16:50,788 When my body was hot and sweating, the blows hurt less than when it was cold in winter 278 00:16:53,662 --> 00:16:55,794 I suffered terribly from thirst 279 00:16:56,412 --> 00:16:58,865 My toes barely touched the ground 280 00:16:58,890 --> 00:17:02,685 And I could hardly reach a bottle of drink 281 00:17:02,710 --> 00:17:06,230 I would bring it close when they gave me a break to drink, from the intensity of thirst 282 00:17:06,255 --> 00:17:07,559 Because of the severe torture 283 00:17:09,036 --> 00:17:11,107 Once, Abu Seif 284 00:17:15,419 --> 00:17:16,419 Told me 285 00:17:17,021 --> 00:17:21,590 You’re a cigarette dealer. I’ll cut you into pieces, put you in bags, and throw you to the dogs 286 00:17:21,615 --> 00:17:26,427 I told him, I’m a Muslim. Neither you nor anyone else will put me in bags and throw me to the dogs 287 00:17:27,732 --> 00:17:30,413 You want, and I want, but God does what He wants 288 00:17:31,247 --> 00:17:38,333 Then he punched me several times. My ribs were broken, and I was exhausted from the beatings, torture, and humiliation 289 00:17:38,849 --> 00:17:41,333 He said, I’ll bring your wife here 290 00:17:43,101 --> 00:17:45,168 I said, You want to bring my wife? 291 00:17:45,615 --> 00:17:49,804 We were created to defend our land, our honor, and our Islam 292 00:17:50,029 --> 00:17:53,279 Who are you to bring my wife to me? I said that to his face 293 00:17:53,708 --> 00:17:56,005 Exactly like that, and even if he were in front of me now 294 00:17:56,279 --> 00:17:59,324 I would confront him with those same words, and I have proof for everything I say 295 00:17:59,349 --> 00:18:01,387 Without a doubt, and there are witnesses to my words 296 00:18:01,646 --> 00:18:03,746 The first witness is God Almighty 297 00:18:03,771 --> 00:18:07,003 And there are 14 people who can testify to what I said 298 00:18:07,857 --> 00:18:12,497 When he said that, I couldn’t bear the way he talked about my wife 299 00:18:12,724 --> 00:18:14,113 So I cursed him 300 00:18:14,138 --> 00:18:17,877 I cursed his honor, even though it’s forbidden in Islam 301 00:18:17,886 --> 00:18:22,487 But I couldn’t bear it when Abu Seif started insulting my honor 302 00:18:23,496 --> 00:18:26,325 Because Abu Seif was a brutal, ignorant type 303 00:18:27,216 --> 00:18:31,314 He had very little understanding or awareness, as we Arabs say 304 00:18:31,865 --> 00:18:33,904 His mind was dull, and he was stupid in his own way 305 00:18:34,654 --> 00:18:40,391 He brought a big stick, about one meter long, and started beating me on my ribs until they broke 306 00:18:40,416 --> 00:18:43,294 I started spitting blood from my mouth 307 00:18:44,130 --> 00:18:46,816 He hit me and caused about 9 wounds on my head 308 00:18:47,325 --> 00:18:50,615 He also hit me here beside my forehead, leaving two visible wounds to this day 309 00:18:51,529 --> 00:18:53,904 So I was left with 9 wounds on my head 310 00:18:55,700 --> 00:18:59,130 Then a Saudi Emir came 311 00:18:59,397 --> 00:19:03,285 May God clothe him with health and reward him, even if he was a member of ISIS 312 00:19:03,310 --> 00:19:06,501 I don’t mean to insult ISIS members in general 313 00:19:07,029 --> 00:19:11,294 Nor to smear their image—except those whom God has spared 314 00:19:11,319 --> 00:19:15,771 There were some who were true Muslims, and when they saw what was happening, they ran away 315 00:19:16,310 --> 00:19:20,424 And some came only to steal, or to distort the image of Islam 316 00:19:20,697 --> 00:19:22,307 And some came for position and power 317 00:19:22,388 --> 00:19:24,107 As for the Saudi, may God guide him 318 00:19:25,536 --> 00:19:30,187 And I pray that my supplication for him reaches the Lord of the Worlds 319 00:19:30,211 --> 00:19:34,718 Because he didn’t help only me; I heard he helped many others too 320 00:19:34,734 --> 00:19:37,685 When he came and saw me hanging in the torture cells 321 00:19:38,952 --> 00:19:41,467 There was also a man with us named Ibrahim al-Fakkash 322 00:19:41,492 --> 00:19:44,927 And another named Ibrahim Ayoub, known as Shantar 323 00:19:44,952 --> 00:19:48,798 And Ibrahim al-Fakkash was nicknamed al-Bariwi. When they saw me being tortured that way 324 00:19:49,536 --> 00:19:51,696 They started screaming and crying for me 325 00:19:53,154 --> 00:19:55,001 And when they began crying for me 326 00:19:56,290 --> 00:20:00,509 The Saudi man—whom I had never met before—heard the shouting and came 327 00:20:01,290 --> 00:20:02,907 He came and unchained me 328 00:20:02,993 --> 00:20:04,793 When he unchained me, Abu Seif came 329 00:20:05,880 --> 00:20:07,805 They argued—who are you, and who sent you? 330 00:20:07,872 --> 00:20:11,193 The Saudi man punched Abu Seif twice and pointed his rifle at him 331 00:20:11,485 --> 00:20:12,485 Then he unchained me 332 00:20:14,189 --> 00:20:15,833 And poured water on me 333 00:20:16,013 --> 00:20:21,029 Then he lowered me to the ground 334 00:20:21,302 --> 00:20:25,185 He and Abu Seif quarreled and complained about each other to the sharia judge 335 00:20:25,201 --> 00:20:27,318 Arguing about how he got in and so on 336 00:20:27,724 --> 00:20:31,458 The second floor was off-limits, reserved for us only 337 00:20:31,739 --> 00:20:38,523 We were sentenced to qisas [execution], and not only us—there were also 5 or 6 women from Raqqa city 338 00:20:38,990 --> 00:20:39,990 From the Rumaila neighborhood 339 00:20:41,771 --> 00:20:45,401 After lowering me, the Saudi man went to bring the sharia committee 340 00:20:46,732 --> 00:20:49,831 And to summon the security and religious officials to show them the torture inside the Hisba prisons 341 00:20:51,169 --> 00:20:52,997 When he came back, they hid me 342 00:20:53,318 --> 00:20:55,839 Every day they hid me in a different prison or location 343 00:20:57,212 --> 00:20:58,821 And one time 344 00:20:59,146 --> 00:21:00,458 They put me in a solitary cell 345 00:21:00,685 --> 00:21:01,966 That solitary cell 346 00:21:03,177 --> 00:21:08,900 Had two walls, one square meter in area, a metal roof, and an iron door 347 00:21:09,724 --> 00:21:11,465 I couldn’t walk 348 00:21:11,490 --> 00:21:15,599 And on top of that, I was chained with iron shackles about one meter long 349 00:21:16,286 --> 00:21:20,325 Tied up, and they would fasten me either to the door itself, its handle, or its ring 350 00:21:20,880 --> 00:21:22,697 Then Abu Zaid, the sharia official, came 351 00:21:25,122 --> 00:21:29,911 May God not prolong his life, nor the lives of his brothers or even his father 352 00:21:29,936 --> 00:21:33,013 Because his father was one of the sheikhs of Raqqa city 353 00:21:33,950 --> 00:21:36,419 One of those sheikhs who change their cloak every day—people with no principles 354 00:21:37,982 --> 00:21:43,106 Abu Zaid came with two other sheikhs, all dressed in white 355 00:21:43,740 --> 00:21:45,816 They carried the air of formal clerics 356 00:21:45,990 --> 00:21:52,060 And I had no strength, relying only on God Almighty and the Quran I held in my hand 357 00:21:53,646 --> 00:21:55,497 He came and said, Prepare yourself 358 00:21:56,271 --> 00:21:59,396 He ordered me to stand, then unlocked my handcuffs and the padlock 359 00:22:00,310 --> 00:22:01,715 He said, Stand 360 00:22:02,443 --> 00:22:06,051 We are going to carry out the hadd [punishment] on you now. Your execution order came today 361 00:22:06,154 --> 00:22:07,708 At 4 p.m. 362 00:22:08,036 --> 00:22:11,880 At the Asr call to prayer, near al-Rahman Mosque intersection on Sajiya Street 363 00:22:14,411 --> 00:22:16,997 The two men with him 364 00:22:18,536 --> 00:22:20,911 Those two were of Saudi origin 365 00:22:23,232 --> 00:22:24,500 They said to him 366 00:22:24,950 --> 00:22:29,396 They said to me, Sheikh, have you repented? I said, Repent from what? Am I an infidel to repent? 367 00:22:30,189 --> 00:22:31,607 Repent from what exactly? 368 00:22:32,974 --> 00:22:35,333 What sin did I commit? 369 00:22:35,833 --> 00:22:37,341 Against the Islamic State? 370 00:22:38,497 --> 00:22:42,626 For what reason does Abu Zaid sentence me to death, and for what crime? 371 00:22:44,325 --> 00:22:46,117 Abu Zaid said to me, Be quiet, be quiet 372 00:22:46,407 --> 00:22:48,786 They told me, You talk too much 373 00:22:49,029 --> 00:22:52,950 They said, You like to talk a lot, and we know you’ve memorized the Book of Monotheism 374 00:22:52,975 --> 00:22:55,126 Yes, I have memorized the Book of Monotheism 375 00:22:56,419 --> 00:23:00,724 I told him, This book of monotheism is not yours—it’s God’s book 376 00:23:00,966 --> 00:23:04,219 And this Quran wasn’t written or revealed by you 377 00:23:04,740 --> 00:23:06,297 So how dare you say that to me 378 00:23:08,529 --> 00:23:12,379 A discussion started between me and him 379 00:23:12,404 --> 00:23:15,997 The other sheikh asked me, and I said, Yes, Sheikh, I used to trade cigarettes 380 00:23:16,716 --> 00:23:19,654 But I repented, and the best of sinners are those who repent 381 00:23:20,661 --> 00:23:24,130 And our Lord, may He be glorified, forgives sins 382 00:23:26,255 --> 00:23:30,316 He began speaking to Abu Zaid, saying, You’re frightening this Muslim man 383 00:23:30,794 --> 00:23:31,809 With execution 384 00:23:32,357 --> 00:23:34,809 He said to him, You people are unjust, and turned against Abu Zaid 385 00:23:35,309 --> 00:23:39,466 He told him, I’ll hold you accountable, Abu Zaid, and I’ll make this man file a complaint against you 386 00:23:39,491 --> 00:23:41,013 We were on the second floor 387 00:23:43,075 --> 00:23:48,575 They argued among themselves, and the man came to me and kissed my forehead 388 00:23:50,497 --> 00:23:53,300 He told me, Rest. God willing, your situation will improve 389 00:23:53,325 --> 00:23:57,136 He asked me, Who beat you like this? I answered, It was Abu Seif and Abu Hudhayfa 390 00:23:58,990 --> 00:24:00,605 After I said that 391 00:24:00,699 --> 00:24:04,496 They came at 11 p.m. and removed my shackles 392 00:24:05,825 --> 00:24:08,263 Then they cuffed my hands behind my back 393 00:24:09,974 --> 00:24:11,918 Once again, Abu Seif with the handcuffs 394 00:24:12,622 --> 00:24:16,583 He grabbed me by the cuffs from above, pulling me up like this 395 00:24:18,263 --> 00:24:19,887 Then lowered me beside the room door 396 00:24:20,075 --> 00:24:22,802 My beard was so long it reached my navel 397 00:24:24,122 --> 00:24:25,816 Now I’ll show you my photo 398 00:24:25,982 --> 00:24:28,808 How I looked with the beard, and how I was sentenced to execution 399 00:24:28,880 --> 00:24:35,590 And how ISIS, the so-called Islamic State, kept my record in their archives 400 00:24:35,615 --> 00:24:38,388 And we don’t generalize—only God knows who was righteous among them 401 00:24:39,200 --> 00:24:41,380 Because the fingers of the hand aren’t all the same 402 00:24:41,904 --> 00:24:44,568 Abu Seif wrapped his hand around my beard 403 00:24:45,568 --> 00:24:49,905 He grabbed my beard—it was long—and dragged me by it while my hands were shackled 404 00:24:50,093 --> 00:24:54,187 I was out of breath, but God Almighty gives a person strength 405 00:24:55,388 --> 00:24:57,202 Great and powerful strength 406 00:24:57,919 --> 00:25:02,732 He kept dragging me by my beard, saying, I’ll teach the whole town a lesson through you 407 00:25:05,122 --> 00:25:08,309 He said to me, plainly, “The time of the Prophet is gone” 408 00:25:08,419 --> 00:25:10,678 I told him, Are we now living in Pharaoh’s time? 409 00:25:11,348 --> 00:25:13,497 For you to say the Prophet’s era has passed? 410 00:25:14,059 --> 00:25:18,958 He dragged me by my beard until he brought me to the feet of Ibrahim al-Fakkash 411 00:25:19,399 --> 00:25:20,805 Who was nicknamed al-Bariwi 412 00:25:20,989 --> 00:25:22,700 He was sentenced along with me too 413 00:25:23,020 --> 00:25:27,297 They told us we’d been sentenced to execution, but the ruling hadn’t been carried out yet 414 00:25:28,094 --> 00:25:30,465 He dragged me by my beard, pulling me along the ground 415 00:25:31,231 --> 00:25:34,617 Until we reached the feet of Ibrahim al-Fakkash, nicknamed al-Bariwi 416 00:25:35,044 --> 00:25:36,516 He asked him, Do you know this man? 417 00:25:36,637 --> 00:25:37,719 He replied, Yes, I know him 418 00:25:37,743 --> 00:25:40,762 Abu Seif said to al-Bariwi, This is the clever player, al-Shuweimi 419 00:25:40,950 --> 00:25:44,669 They called me the clever player because they never caught me with cigarettes 420 00:25:44,700 --> 00:25:50,786 I’d been in so many prisons and detentions—arrested 27 times before by the Hisba (religious police) 421 00:25:50,989 --> 00:25:55,215 Once they detained me for security reasons, and another time I spent a month and a half in Point 11 422 00:25:56,161 --> 00:25:58,270 That’s why they gave me that nickname 423 00:25:58,872 --> 00:26:03,591 He dragged me by my beard all that distance, pulled me out of the room again, still dragging me 424 00:26:07,950 --> 00:26:12,536 I stayed with them in the investigation section for about 53 or 54 days 425 00:26:14,029 --> 00:26:15,029 They came 426 00:26:15,926 --> 00:26:18,163 And shackled my hands behind my back again 427 00:26:19,295 --> 00:26:21,716 They took me out after 53 days 428 00:26:21,889 --> 00:26:22,889 And said to me 429 00:26:23,536 --> 00:26:27,310 We’ll spare your neck if you pay a sum of money 430 00:26:28,848 --> 00:26:30,864 In exchange for paying a financial amount 431 00:26:31,311 --> 00:26:36,309 The ruling would be treated like theft but under a religious ruling 432 00:26:36,919 --> 00:26:39,805 In the past, people would say things like, I’ll rob you right before your eyes 433 00:26:40,083 --> 00:26:42,016 My strength and my dignity 434 00:26:42,465 --> 00:26:45,684 They wanted to rob me, but in the name of what the Prophet said—meaning freeing a slave 435 00:26:45,709 --> 00:26:50,161 They asked me, What property do you have? At that time, I owned 6 or 7 cars 436 00:26:50,981 --> 00:26:54,481 Because I worked hard in the cigarette trade 437 00:26:55,262 --> 00:26:58,110 Whether that work was forbidden or not 438 00:26:58,575 --> 00:27:01,144 Some people said it was forbidden, others said it wasn’t 439 00:27:01,169 --> 00:27:06,144 Maybe we were ignorant about it, but we didn’t really know whether it was forbidden or not 440 00:27:06,169 --> 00:27:09,126 The important thing is, I worked hard, forbidden or not 441 00:27:09,317 --> 00:27:11,602 I struggled and earned that money 442 00:27:12,208 --> 00:27:17,493 They took everything I had—gold, silver, and cars 443 00:27:18,094 --> 00:27:19,981 They took everything, even my farmland 444 00:27:20,262 --> 00:27:22,489 I had a deed of ownership from 50 years ago 445 00:27:23,129 --> 00:27:24,875 In my name, inherited from my father 446 00:27:24,900 --> 00:27:29,126 Back then, the Syrian regime used to seize land from people who owned a lot of it 447 00:27:29,645 --> 00:27:33,281 Out of fear, my father registered the land in his sons’ names 448 00:27:33,306 --> 00:27:37,001 He divided the land equally among his sons 449 00:27:38,020 --> 00:27:40,098 So my share was the same as my brothers’ 450 00:27:41,103 --> 00:27:43,786 Even the land that was originally my father’s 451 00:27:44,197 --> 00:27:46,059 They made me fingerprint it as sold and bought 452 00:27:46,084 --> 00:27:49,019 I also had a motorbike with two frames at times 453 00:27:49,044 --> 00:27:53,789 I used it around the neighborhood and inside the city during traffic jams and hot weather 454 00:27:53,814 --> 00:27:56,529 They made me fingerprint that as sold and bought too 455 00:28:01,311 --> 00:28:04,923 I was detained many times, and regarding the interrogation sessions 456 00:28:05,491 --> 00:28:10,225 They sometimes lasted long; sometimes they interrogated me, sometimes they didn’t 457 00:28:10,884 --> 00:28:12,756 Just to put psychological pressure on me 458 00:28:12,764 --> 00:28:15,326 My first interrogation was at Point 11 459 00:28:16,197 --> 00:28:20,603 I talked too much during the interrogation because of how unjustly they treated me 460 00:28:21,095 --> 00:28:25,415 I was innocent—I was just trying to earn a living, working as a taxi driver 461 00:28:25,876 --> 00:28:29,150 Because I talked so much, they interrogated me and took me to the judge 462 00:28:29,175 --> 00:28:31,929 Their system was that every week they brought us a blank sheet 463 00:28:31,954 --> 00:28:34,595 We’d write our names, like Talal Mustafa al-Shuweimi 464 00:28:34,907 --> 00:28:37,790 Was he interrogated? If yes, he should write “yes” 465 00:28:38,181 --> 00:28:40,039 If not interrogated, he should write “no” 466 00:28:40,064 --> 00:28:41,900 And was he presented to the judge? “Yes” or “no” 467 00:28:41,925 --> 00:28:46,204 And every time, I wrote “no,” blaming them for my case 468 00:28:47,064 --> 00:28:51,003 Since I had children, no means, and my house was rented in the beginning 469 00:28:52,308 --> 00:28:54,958 Sometimes they’d take me to the prison corridor 470 00:28:55,675 --> 00:28:57,105 At Point 11 471 00:28:58,379 --> 00:29:02,137 They’d leave me for two or three hours, blindfolded 472 00:29:02,731 --> 00:29:03,731 Standing on my feet 473 00:29:04,379 --> 00:29:06,426 Or sitting on the ground 474 00:29:06,598 --> 00:29:08,551 They wouldn’t speak to me or beat me 475 00:29:08,576 --> 00:29:11,870 But I could hear the sounds of torture and beatings in the corridor 476 00:29:11,895 --> 00:29:16,401 They’d shout at the prisoners, Stand up, you dog! Turn around! Lift your foot! They had their own methods of torture 477 00:29:17,198 --> 00:29:24,317 But they didn’t beat me; they just kept me in the corridor blindfolded 478 00:29:24,786 --> 00:29:26,794 Leaving me that way for two or three hours 479 00:29:26,819 --> 00:29:29,232 In the end, he’d say to me, Sheikh 480 00:29:29,257 --> 00:29:34,544 Go back to your group cell. I’d remove the blindfold inside and hand it to him through the window 481 00:29:34,622 --> 00:29:38,262 That happened about 10 times 482 00:29:39,194 --> 00:29:40,512 And the last time 483 00:29:42,629 --> 00:29:47,465 I told them I wanted to be interrogated because I was honestly afraid they’d put me in the corridor again 484 00:29:48,145 --> 00:29:49,809 And one time 485 00:29:49,999 --> 00:29:51,239 I lost my patience 486 00:29:51,551 --> 00:29:53,762 I started crying and said, It’s only one death 487 00:29:53,950 --> 00:29:55,873 A person only dies once 488 00:29:56,833 --> 00:30:00,590 I started crying and shouting, so they blindfolded me and took me away 489 00:30:01,395 --> 00:30:03,325 They took me to the interrogation room 490 00:30:03,934 --> 00:30:05,083 And the interrogator 491 00:30:06,520 --> 00:30:10,129 He died, may God have mercy on him 492 00:30:10,481 --> 00:30:11,825 His name was Abu Abdul Rahman 493 00:30:12,786 --> 00:30:15,301 He was from the Jaif area in Raqqa, from the Ghanem al-Zahir clan 494 00:30:16,739 --> 00:30:18,373 He said to me, Talal al-Shuweimi 495 00:30:21,223 --> 00:30:22,223 You 496 00:30:22,801 --> 00:30:24,246 Are you still doing amulet sorcery? 497 00:30:24,270 --> 00:30:31,387 Since I’m from the Marandiyya clan, and our ancestors were known for religion and for using drums 498 00:30:32,333 --> 00:30:38,604 He said, Do you still make amulet spells and play the drums? 499 00:30:38,870 --> 00:30:41,213 And I knew him. I knew him well 500 00:30:42,495 --> 00:30:43,901 I told him, I swear, Sheikh 501 00:30:44,784 --> 00:30:48,705 I told him these words when you stop playing the rababa [string instrument] 502 00:30:49,487 --> 00:30:52,924 He said to me, Are you the brother of Abdul Rahim Shuweimi? 503 00:30:53,370 --> 00:30:54,705 I said, Yes, I’m his brother 504 00:30:55,143 --> 00:30:56,604 I said, But, Sheikh 505 00:30:57,854 --> 00:30:59,319 At that time, my family was trying to use mediations for me 506 00:30:59,344 --> 00:31:05,032 My mother, an old woman, wherever she saw a clan sheikh or a member of ISIS 507 00:31:05,057 --> 00:31:10,114 Any man with a long beard, she would go to him and say, My son has children, and so on 508 00:31:10,901 --> 00:31:13,432 She would follow them around for her son, hoping they’d release me, and I had children 509 00:31:13,456 --> 00:31:17,168 My children became my parents’ responsibility, and they had no power to help 510 00:31:18,809 --> 00:31:21,595 We argued about this topic 511 00:31:21,924 --> 00:31:23,680 Then he said to me, You’re a filthy person 512 00:31:23,705 --> 00:31:27,537 I said, So now for you the Muslim is filthy, and Muslims deserve execution 513 00:31:28,065 --> 00:31:30,698 It’s the rule of the strong over the weak, and there’s no power or strength 514 00:31:31,627 --> 00:31:34,259 He said to me, God willing, you won’t leave this place 515 00:31:34,884 --> 00:31:37,056 He brought in a man, and that man 516 00:31:38,337 --> 00:31:40,735 Had a medium-sized passenger van 517 00:31:41,556 --> 00:31:45,009 His house was in the Kalta area in Raqqa’s countryside, and he used to transport female teachers 518 00:31:45,634 --> 00:31:51,706 The group forced teachers to attend repentance courses. The point is, he used to drive those teachers 519 00:31:52,251 --> 00:31:55,939 The group accused him of smuggling people out of Raqqa 520 00:31:56,532 --> 00:31:58,414 They brought the man and blindfolded him 521 00:31:59,821 --> 00:32:01,712 That act was meant as punishment for me 522 00:32:02,134 --> 00:32:04,910 I felt it was a punishment for me, and while he was talking to me 523 00:32:05,501 --> 00:32:06,782 He made me sit on a chair 524 00:32:07,665 --> 00:32:08,798 While I was blindfolded 525 00:32:10,243 --> 00:32:16,384 I was only blindfolded and tied with plastic ties 526 00:32:16,409 --> 00:32:18,618 The kind used for bundling electric cables 527 00:32:20,525 --> 00:32:26,509 Then he brought the man and started beating him with a water hose called “Lakhdar Brahimi” 528 00:32:26,697 --> 00:32:28,485 He disfigured the man’s body with the beating 529 00:32:28,510 --> 00:32:30,190 The man cried intensely 530 00:32:31,018 --> 00:32:36,402 I could hear the hose striking near me, and he kept beating him until he killed him 531 00:32:37,105 --> 00:32:40,894 Finally, I was so upset that I said, He’ll kill this man if he continues 532 00:32:41,579 --> 00:32:46,407 I told him, I don’t want the interrogation anymore, just stop hitting him, because I realized he was doing it for my sake 533 00:32:46,699 --> 00:32:49,634 He couldn’t hit me, not out of fear 534 00:32:50,116 --> 00:32:53,164 But because there was no proof against me, so he had no right to beat me 535 00:32:53,189 --> 00:32:57,290 And because Abu Yousuf the interrogator and Abu Suhaib the Tunisian were above him in rank 536 00:32:57,315 --> 00:33:02,415 Although Abu Suhaib the Tunisian was very strict religiously, he never allowed anyone to be beaten 537 00:33:02,922 --> 00:33:05,211 He wouldn’t allow it, and that’s the truth 538 00:33:05,689 --> 00:33:10,289 He’s gone now, but may God reward him; that Tunisian man never allowed anyone to be beaten 539 00:33:12,852 --> 00:33:16,971 He disfigured the man. In the end, I told him, Don’t beat him to scare me, I don’t want the interrogation 540 00:33:16,996 --> 00:33:19,805 From now on, I won’t say I want interrogation or a judge 541 00:33:20,019 --> 00:33:21,388 Just leave the man alone 542 00:33:22,735 --> 00:33:25,094 I felt guilty, that I was the reason he was beaten 543 00:33:25,119 --> 00:33:28,656 Then Abu Yousuf came in to us 544 00:33:28,665 --> 00:33:31,147 He pulled me back into the group cell and said, Next time someone asks you 545 00:33:31,172 --> 00:33:33,430 Tell him you were interrogated and brought before the judge 546 00:33:33,438 --> 00:33:35,297 I said, Thank you, brother, may God guide you 547 00:33:36,555 --> 00:33:39,722 They took me into the cell, and they brought the man into my room 548 00:33:40,250 --> 00:33:41,805 In the first room, of course 549 00:33:42,877 --> 00:33:43,877 And I sat down 550 00:33:45,315 --> 00:33:47,531 When I saw the man, by God Almighty 551 00:33:47,790 --> 00:33:49,470 One of the hose strikes 552 00:33:49,993 --> 00:33:51,727 When he was suspended like this 553 00:33:52,188 --> 00:33:54,227 With an iron rod 554 00:33:54,586 --> 00:33:58,734 A thick iron rod, and his toes barely touched the ground 555 00:33:59,008 --> 00:34:04,008 The poor man was terrified; when they hit his chest, he lowered his head 556 00:34:04,119 --> 00:34:06,049 Then one of the hose strikes hit his face 557 00:34:06,665 --> 00:34:10,509 His face swelled painfully, and his eyes were no longer visible 558 00:34:11,204 --> 00:34:12,947 I truly felt 559 00:34:14,807 --> 00:34:16,461 That I carried that man’s guilt 560 00:34:16,486 --> 00:34:20,010 That same day, they brought a man from the al-Shanani family, a licorice drink seller 561 00:34:22,603 --> 00:34:24,931 Interviewer: Can you tell us their names if you know them? 562 00:34:25,463 --> 00:34:31,319 That man was from the Kalta area, but honestly, I don’t know his exact name 563 00:34:31,426 --> 00:34:36,118 But he was known as Abu Ahmad, and he drove a light-brown passenger car 564 00:34:36,478 --> 00:34:38,321 He still owns that car today 565 00:34:38,478 --> 00:34:40,078 But the man called al-Shanani 566 00:34:40,103 --> 00:34:43,804 His name was probably Mustafa al-Shanani, also called Abu Firas al-Shanani, a well-known man 567 00:34:43,829 --> 00:34:45,320 At the intersection of the Sayf al-Dawla area 568 00:34:45,696 --> 00:34:52,391 With February 23 Street in Raqqa city. They’ve been selling licorice for generations, known as the Shanani family 569 00:34:52,477 --> 00:34:53,696 They brought the old man 570 00:34:54,308 --> 00:34:58,917 When they brought him into Point 11, the old man had no skin left on his back 571 00:34:59,181 --> 00:35:00,187 Only bone remained 572 00:35:00,212 --> 00:35:03,235 I spent more than 25 days treating the old man 573 00:35:03,260 --> 00:35:05,118 And to this day, he and I are friends 574 00:35:05,485 --> 00:35:07,393 He tells me, How can I repay you? 575 00:35:07,418 --> 00:35:13,489 And I say I did nothing. I just helped treat him, using the group’s medicines since he was elderly 576 00:35:13,610 --> 00:35:17,614 At that time, they detained him and his son, whose name was Firas 577 00:35:18,403 --> 00:35:19,915 He was in the second group cell 578 00:35:20,243 --> 00:35:21,681 And they broke his leg 579 00:35:22,095 --> 00:35:24,598 Firas is still alive 580 00:35:25,056 --> 00:35:27,399 Those who conducted the interrogations with us 581 00:35:28,556 --> 00:35:30,321 Were from inside the prison 582 00:35:30,446 --> 00:35:33,526 Like Abu Yousuf the interrogator and Abu Abdul Rahman 583 00:35:33,712 --> 00:35:35,595 The second one was from the al-Sahou clan 584 00:35:36,089 --> 00:35:40,401 The group deliberately used people in interrogations who had strong intelligence connections inside the city 585 00:35:41,665 --> 00:35:44,399 That’s one point, and the first and most important 586 00:35:44,892 --> 00:35:46,634 The most important and strongest thing they had 587 00:35:47,337 --> 00:35:49,603 Was that they had security agents inside the prison 588 00:35:50,009 --> 00:35:53,251 They had security agents from our own people, from our towns, sadly 589 00:35:53,321 --> 00:35:56,790 ISIS had security agents from our local people working inside the prison for money 590 00:35:57,274 --> 00:36:01,306 For example, someone would come, and I’d look at him, and one of them 591 00:36:01,860 --> 00:36:04,017 Was from the al-Bou Saraya clan, named Abdullah 592 00:36:04,259 --> 00:36:07,204 He sat beside me, and I had been in prison for 10 days 593 00:36:09,478 --> 00:36:15,501 When he sat and started crying, saying he was wronged and jailed over selling a pistol 594 00:36:15,563 --> 00:36:18,892 I trusted him, as I had nothing to hide 595 00:36:19,368 --> 00:36:22,712 The group’s informants or security men 596 00:36:22,938 --> 00:36:28,310 Would come acting like victims, saying they were innocent, and talk about ISIS in front of me, saying we’ll get out tomorrow 597 00:36:28,415 --> 00:36:31,732 And say, God is generous, and promise that such-and-such faction would enter the city 598 00:36:31,845 --> 00:36:35,001 So the person in front of them would confess and start talking 599 00:36:35,079 --> 00:36:40,306 These were the ones ISIS sent in as informants and internal agents 600 00:36:40,412 --> 00:36:42,217 They were very intelligent people 601 00:36:42,795 --> 00:36:46,193 One of these people was named Abdullah al-Asaad 602 00:36:46,508 --> 00:36:50,407 His house was next to the Nabhan Glass Shop in Raqqa, and he was killed 603 00:36:51,889 --> 00:36:55,029 While fighting in one of the battles 604 00:36:55,392 --> 00:36:58,717 He started crying in front of me, and about 40 days later 605 00:36:59,115 --> 00:37:01,904 He started crying again and told me that his mother had died 606 00:37:02,946 --> 00:37:06,912 When I got out of prison, his goal was to get information from me 607 00:37:07,376 --> 00:37:10,986 When I was released, I went to his house to offer condolences to his father 608 00:37:11,011 --> 00:37:14,193 And I said, Keep your faith strong, Abdullah is crying and mourning his mother 609 00:37:14,218 --> 00:37:17,767 Since I had been released, it was my duty to visit my friend’s family who was still in prison 610 00:37:17,792 --> 00:37:22,826 I was shocked when his mother came out, cursing him and saying, May God never grant him success, and that she had disowned him 611 00:37:23,058 --> 00:37:25,602 She said, He claims I’m dead, but he’s an informant for the group 612 00:37:26,811 --> 00:37:30,939 There were many people who infiltrated others in this same way 613 00:37:31,894 --> 00:37:34,011 Our people are kind-hearted and simple 614 00:37:34,487 --> 00:37:38,004 So they might slip and say something by mistake, and that security informant 615 00:37:38,581 --> 00:37:39,581 Would then 616 00:37:40,042 --> 00:37:45,097 Report it to the security forces, and based on that, the person would be accused. Many people were killed that way 617 00:37:46,175 --> 00:37:48,011 Stories like this were common 618 00:37:52,114 --> 00:37:54,332 As for torture methods 619 00:37:55,324 --> 00:37:56,324 Torture 620 00:37:57,668 --> 00:37:59,928 We witnessed how they tortured people 621 00:38:00,926 --> 00:38:02,404 The problem was that they had two styles 622 00:38:02,847 --> 00:38:04,420 One underground 623 00:38:04,637 --> 00:38:06,412 And another above ground, completely different 624 00:38:07,301 --> 00:38:12,215 Like a person with two faces—and both faces were terrifying 625 00:38:12,761 --> 00:38:15,006 As for the Hisba (religious police) 626 00:38:16,125 --> 00:38:17,624 It had 20 faces 627 00:38:17,771 --> 00:38:19,477 Regarding torture methods 628 00:38:19,998 --> 00:38:25,013 Anything you can imagine existed. Many locals were imprisoned 629 00:38:25,560 --> 00:38:30,326 And subjected to different torture methods—for example, crucifixion 630 00:38:31,305 --> 00:38:34,959 Or they’d shackle your hands like this with cuffs 631 00:38:35,388 --> 00:38:39,615 When your hands were raised with the cuffs, your ribs would tear apart from here 632 00:38:39,943 --> 00:38:43,795 When Abu Seif lifted the prisoner—he was a huge man from eating too much Western food 633 00:38:44,003 --> 00:38:47,050 God gave him the strength to eat 4 or 5 sandwiches 634 00:38:47,246 --> 00:38:49,386 At the expense of the so-called caliphate 635 00:38:49,605 --> 00:38:52,043 He was very strong, while I was weak 636 00:38:52,379 --> 00:38:57,456 He would grab me by the neck and lift me about 20 or 30 centimeters off the ground 637 00:38:57,481 --> 00:39:00,465 My legs would kick violently, just like in American movies 638 00:39:00,871 --> 00:39:05,019 I couldn’t walk or breathe from the pain in my chest 639 00:39:05,457 --> 00:39:11,096 I had tonsillitis, my ribs were broken, and to this day I still suffer chest pain 640 00:39:11,347 --> 00:39:13,020 At that time 641 00:39:13,457 --> 00:39:18,988 The guys, God bless them, took care of me for about 68 days 642 00:39:19,430 --> 00:39:25,055 They would take me to the toilets, bathe me, and cared for me well 643 00:39:25,119 --> 00:39:29,923 Among them were Ibrahim Abu Nashwa and Ibrahim Ayyoub, known as Shantar 644 00:39:30,342 --> 00:39:34,529 All the guys who were with me in prison were kind and helped me in every way 645 00:39:34,730 --> 00:39:40,666 I was never cared for in my life the way those six men cared for me when I was the seventh 646 00:39:40,691 --> 00:39:43,404 When I was broken and my ribs were shattered in prison 647 00:39:47,662 --> 00:39:49,182 Out of fear and terror 648 00:39:49,660 --> 00:39:53,115 My ribs and legs could barely move 649 00:39:53,496 --> 00:39:58,972 In the room above us there were solitary cells, each about one square meter, with a ceiling 650 00:39:59,465 --> 00:40:01,715 Barriwi carried me and we went up 651 00:40:02,425 --> 00:40:06,222 He broke a small iron piece from one of the solitary cells 652 00:40:09,183 --> 00:40:12,158 Then Barriwi started scraping the wall 653 00:40:12,183 --> 00:40:14,433 The wall was coated with smooth cement 654 00:40:14,458 --> 00:40:18,121 We discovered it was made of modern white stone, not black stone 655 00:40:19,496 --> 00:40:23,715 Barriwi said, “Talal al-Shuweimi, let’s escape,” so we tried to run away 656 00:40:24,399 --> 00:40:25,813 It was me and Barriwi 657 00:40:26,230 --> 00:40:33,636 And Ibrahim Ayyoub, known as Shantar, was watching the door with Bara al-Ano 658 00:40:35,400 --> 00:40:40,918 We started digging the wall and dug the length of about two bricks and the width of two more 659 00:40:41,697 --> 00:40:45,800 But we were terrified, because each brick 660 00:40:46,566 --> 00:40:49,097 Had two sides, one of them thick 661 00:40:49,871 --> 00:40:55,877 The thick side was pressed against another thick side, with a cement layer in between that couldn’t be broken easily—it made a loud sound 662 00:40:55,902 --> 00:41:01,683 Across from our room, one of the group’s members was standing guard 663 00:41:03,159 --> 00:41:04,286 We were terrified 664 00:41:04,753 --> 00:41:09,511 I went to the sheikh and told him, Out of fear, no one knows what to do—especially the sentenced ones 665 00:41:09,903 --> 00:41:11,722 So I said to the imprisoned sheikh with us 666 00:41:12,160 --> 00:41:13,325 My brother 667 00:41:14,410 --> 00:41:20,105 Read to us from the Quran, maybe God will save us from them and help us escape 668 00:41:20,528 --> 00:41:25,902 Or make them blind to us—things like that, which are close to sorcery, God forbid 669 00:41:25,962 --> 00:41:29,830 But we were desperate, and a drowning man clings to a straw 670 00:41:30,376 --> 00:41:35,526 As you know, I was sentenced to execution, and a drowning man clings not just to a straw but to the water itself 671 00:41:36,111 --> 00:41:37,517 Trying to hold onto the water 672 00:41:38,392 --> 00:41:41,125 The sheikh actually believed himself 673 00:41:41,150 --> 00:41:44,344 He was a man whose house was on Mansour Street in Raqqa, sentenced to execution 674 00:41:44,369 --> 00:41:46,119 On charges of sorcery 675 00:41:46,923 --> 00:41:49,830 He said he treated people with traditional Arabic medicine 676 00:41:50,486 --> 00:41:53,509 He went to treat a woman who had joint pain in her legs 677 00:41:53,798 --> 00:41:56,511 And they arrested him for that, accusing him of witchcraft 678 00:41:56,964 --> 00:41:58,602 That man started reciting 679 00:41:59,620 --> 00:42:05,003 And it became like some satanic ritual, while we continued pounding on the wall 680 00:42:05,573 --> 00:42:08,917 Hard, but the guard outside didn’t hear us 681 00:42:09,581 --> 00:42:14,589 The guard sitting outside was an ISIS member who had just finished his training course 682 00:42:15,066 --> 00:42:16,323 His name was Abu al-Yaman 683 00:42:16,730 --> 00:42:19,011 Abu al-Yaman was from Aleppo Province 684 00:42:21,566 --> 00:42:25,417 When we made an opening in the wall, one of us, a diabetic man 685 00:42:25,442 --> 00:42:28,206 Named Ibrahim al-Ali al-Alkan, known as Abu Nashwa 686 00:42:28,620 --> 00:42:31,518 Got so scared that his blood sugar spiked 687 00:42:32,433 --> 00:42:33,730 He almost died 688 00:42:34,699 --> 00:42:35,925 And he actually did die 689 00:42:36,612 --> 00:42:38,901 They had brought us wool blankets 690 00:42:39,894 --> 00:42:42,800 Because winter was approaching 691 00:42:43,558 --> 00:42:47,948 We tore up those wool blankets, and also some other covers 692 00:42:48,276 --> 00:42:52,198 When we broke through the wall of the Hisba prison, behind it was an investigation building on Sajiyah Street 693 00:42:52,737 --> 00:42:55,940 There was a depth of about 7 meters 694 00:42:56,214 --> 00:42:58,698 After 7 meters, there was the yard of a ground-floor house 695 00:42:59,847 --> 00:43:02,659 Because of the pounding noise, the homeowner came out 696 00:43:02,894 --> 00:43:07,394 He shouted, Who are you? I said, Uncle, I’m Talal al-Shuweimi, your relative 697 00:43:07,511 --> 00:43:11,901 And Ibrahim al-Fakkash said, I’m from the Fakkash family, from the Shibli Salamah clan 698 00:43:12,026 --> 00:43:16,925 We started crying and begged him not to report us—we were sentenced to execution 699 00:43:17,245 --> 00:43:19,417 The man cried along with us 700 00:43:19,987 --> 00:43:23,839 He started crying and said, My nephews, jump down quickly 701 00:43:24,566 --> 00:43:26,878 So they wouldn’t cut off my neck with yours 702 00:43:28,605 --> 00:43:30,816 And the man was holding a laser lighter for us 703 00:43:31,058 --> 00:43:35,397 He looked at us and said, “My brothers’ sons, hurry up, the daylight has broken,” and when we finished 704 00:43:36,620 --> 00:43:39,201 Ibrahim al-Alkan fell ill 705 00:43:39,683 --> 00:43:41,019 And the last time we went out 706 00:43:41,386 --> 00:43:44,839 The house of Emir Abu Seif was on Sajiyah Street 707 00:43:45,917 --> 00:43:49,593 The Hisba (religious police) base was on Sajiyah Street, and the road was like this 708 00:43:49,797 --> 00:43:53,312 Sajiyah Street is on the southern side 709 00:43:53,526 --> 00:43:56,921 The Hisba is on the northern side, and Abu Seif’s house is on Sajiyah Street 710 00:43:56,980 --> 00:43:59,501 The organization had taken that house from a Kurdish family 711 00:43:59,526 --> 00:44:05,679 The owner was called Abu Sateef, a well-known bicycle repairman in Raqqa and on Sajiyah Street 712 00:44:05,773 --> 00:44:07,507 So Abu Seif took his house 713 00:44:08,128 --> 00:44:12,906 Abu Seif, the Emir of the Investigation, took the house of Abu Sateef, the Kurdish bicycle repairman 714 00:44:13,305 --> 00:44:15,932 The Hisba base was across from his house, and when he went out 715 00:44:18,245 --> 00:44:22,706 He saw that the wall had been destroyed across the width of about two and a half rooms 716 00:44:23,476 --> 00:44:27,800 Suddenly, the security men surrounded the area and came up to us 717 00:44:29,008 --> 00:44:35,198 They started shooting at my feet and separated me from the other young men 718 00:44:35,565 --> 00:44:37,104 Then they brought us back again 719 00:44:37,659 --> 00:44:42,425 For the case of crucifixion and suspension, and of course the beatings and insults 720 00:44:46,823 --> 00:44:49,526 I was tortured and kept being tortured for 3 more days 721 00:44:50,550 --> 00:44:53,807 After 3 days, they took me to the court 722 00:44:56,393 --> 00:45:02,495 There was an air fan in the room where they put us 723 00:45:02,706 --> 00:45:05,908 It was installed in the wall for air and odor ventilation, called the sharaq [door vent] 724 00:45:06,893 --> 00:45:08,425 I told the guys, “Listen, guys 725 00:45:10,979 --> 00:45:13,026 If they take us down to the same room 726 00:45:13,393 --> 00:45:16,041 The sharaq switch is below, and we’re placed upstairs in the solitary cells 727 00:45:16,066 --> 00:45:18,620 When you turn on the metal sharaq, it will make noise 728 00:45:19,050 --> 00:45:24,721 Whoever among you goes down to our old room, turn it on so we know who was sent there” 729 00:45:25,909 --> 00:45:27,706 The first one they summoned was al-Bariwi 730 00:45:29,237 --> 00:45:33,522 We waited 5 minutes, 15 minutes, half an hour, and the sharaq still didn’t turn on 731 00:45:35,182 --> 00:45:39,229 Then they summoned another person too 732 00:45:40,050 --> 00:45:41,800 And the sharaq still didn’t turn on 733 00:45:43,108 --> 00:45:45,706 Fear and terror spread among us 734 00:45:46,917 --> 00:45:48,026 Then they called my name, Talal al-Shuweimi 735 00:45:48,051 --> 00:45:52,518 They shackled me and blindfolded me. Abu Seif lifted me by my shoulder and led me 736 00:45:53,424 --> 00:45:55,456 And Abu Hudhayfa held me by the other shoulder 737 00:45:57,284 --> 00:46:04,190 They carried me forcefully as if they were about to carry out the sentence against me and took me out 738 00:46:06,026 --> 00:46:07,673 They said, “Stand,” so I stood 739 00:46:08,573 --> 00:46:12,024 He asked me, “Are you so-and-so?” I said, “Yes, I am” 740 00:46:13,148 --> 00:46:18,206 He said, “You did this and that.” I said, “Yes, Sheikh, I was the biggest cigarette trader 741 00:46:18,698 --> 00:46:22,923 Under the caliphate, but God guided me, and I quit over a year or a year and a half ago” 742 00:46:22,948 --> 00:46:27,011 “And now, after I quit the trade, you summon me and treat me like this?” 743 00:46:27,948 --> 00:46:30,701 We argued, and the sheikh started insulting me 744 00:46:31,268 --> 00:46:36,995 He said, “You’re talkative and loud. You think religion is a game,” and so on 745 00:46:37,378 --> 00:46:41,518 He said, “You and that woman—I won’t mention her name— 746 00:46:41,979 --> 00:46:44,763 You both smoke hookah and walk around with that other woman” 747 00:46:44,788 --> 00:46:47,496 They were all women suffering from poverty and hunger 748 00:46:48,013 --> 00:46:51,588 They traded cigarettes to earn a living and support their children 749 00:46:51,613 --> 00:46:55,765 Some were widows, others had children, and one had a disabled child 750 00:46:55,796 --> 00:46:58,972 They relied on God and borrowed cigarettes from me 751 00:46:58,997 --> 00:47:03,097 To sell them, cover their expenses, and provide for their families 752 00:47:04,488 --> 00:47:05,589 So I said to him, “Sheikh 753 00:47:06,644 --> 00:47:10,253 You’re accusing me of false charges 754 00:47:10,613 --> 00:47:14,550 Keep the cigarette charge; it’s better than accusing me of adultery 755 00:47:15,253 --> 00:47:19,900 Imagine, I entered prison for cigarette trading and ended up with 10 other charges I had nothing to do with 756 00:47:19,925 --> 00:47:23,019 And sadly, I didn’t even know about them until the very end 757 00:47:23,261 --> 00:47:27,988 In the end, I learned that the cases I was sentenced for had nothing to do with cigarettes 758 00:47:28,144 --> 00:47:30,823 My arrest and interrogation were indeed for cigarette trading 759 00:47:31,244 --> 00:47:34,561 But eventually, none of the charges were related to that 760 00:47:34,586 --> 00:47:37,206 Even in the same court, they accused me of adultery 761 00:47:37,933 --> 00:47:41,042 With one of the women who sold cigarettes 762 00:47:41,067 --> 00:47:48,826 Now tell me, does it make sense? I have 3 wives, my daughters are unmarried, and I have 13 children 763 00:47:49,339 --> 00:47:50,698 And now I’m going to commit adultery? 764 00:47:51,370 --> 00:47:54,738 After 3 wives and after reaching 40 or 45 years old? 765 00:47:54,972 --> 00:47:56,798 I told him, “Sheikh, that’s not logical 766 00:47:57,014 --> 00:47:59,896 Leave me with the cigarette trading charge, may God guide you 767 00:48:00,107 --> 00:48:02,902 Judge me for that—it’s better than judging me for adultery” 768 00:48:04,238 --> 00:48:07,529 I told him, “If you don’t fear God, then you should be feared.” He said, “Do you think we don’t fear God?” 769 00:48:07,620 --> 00:48:10,795 Then he stood up and hit me. I don’t know with what, because I was blindfolded 770 00:48:10,855 --> 00:48:15,441 I felt something running down my head, like heat 771 00:48:17,964 --> 00:48:20,800 And that’s when he pronounced the sentence 772 00:48:21,534 --> 00:48:28,482 He didn’t give me anything, didn’t even let me sit. He just asked me a few harsh questions 773 00:48:28,608 --> 00:48:30,894 Then said, “We sentence you to qisas [retribution] 774 00:48:32,159 --> 00:48:33,314 You did such and such” 775 00:48:33,339 --> 00:48:35,003 He asked, “What do you think of my sentence?” 776 00:48:35,363 --> 00:48:38,109 At that moment, I mocked the judgment 777 00:48:38,636 --> 00:48:42,719 I didn’t take it seriously. I was just a cigarette trader—what does qisas have to do with that? 778 00:48:43,305 --> 00:48:46,503 Why should I be sentenced to retribution for cigarette trading? 779 00:48:47,777 --> 00:48:51,105 They judged me as a corrupter on earth 780 00:48:51,902 --> 00:48:54,496 But “corruption on earth” means armed robbery and banditry 781 00:48:55,300 --> 00:48:57,816 Armed assault—not cigarette trading 782 00:48:59,339 --> 00:49:02,198 And today, the whole world smokes cigarettes 783 00:49:03,089 --> 00:49:04,995 Everyone smokes cigarettes 784 00:49:06,128 --> 00:49:08,316 Even alcohol existed in the time of the Companions 785 00:49:08,605 --> 00:49:11,167 But now, in the time of these “new Companions,” 786 00:49:11,792 --> 00:49:14,105 They want to cut off heads and hands 787 00:49:14,792 --> 00:49:18,589 But there’s no power or strength left—today, they’re strong and we’re weak 788 00:49:19,839 --> 00:49:21,206 He asked me, “What do you say about the judgment?” 789 00:49:21,498 --> 00:49:24,318 I said, “Me?”—I wasn’t afraid, I was relaxed 790 00:49:25,042 --> 00:49:27,550 I wasn’t scared at all; I was at ease 791 00:49:28,238 --> 00:49:33,222 I told him, “I’m satisfied with your judgment.” When I said that, they were surprised 792 00:49:33,816 --> 00:49:38,206 He said, “Why are you satisfied?” I said, “Isn’t this God’s decree, the Almighty?” 793 00:49:38,602 --> 00:49:42,113 “You’re ruling by God’s sharia, and I’m content with God’s law” 794 00:49:42,738 --> 00:49:43,800 Then he said to me 795 00:49:44,519 --> 00:49:46,214 “What will you say to the seven-member committee?” 796 00:49:46,488 --> 00:49:51,406 I said, “Who are the seven-member committee?” He said, “They’re the sheikhs, because your file will be sent to them” 797 00:49:52,839 --> 00:49:55,222 I said, “What difference will my words make?” 798 00:49:55,247 --> 00:49:59,831 “You already sentenced me to qisas—what can those sheikhs do now?” 799 00:50:01,292 --> 00:50:04,519 He said, “Say whatever you want to tell them” 800 00:50:04,545 --> 00:50:09,628 I said, “Send them my regards and tell them the Sheikh’s judgment is false upon falsehood” 801 00:50:12,105 --> 00:50:13,795 Your entire verdict is invalid upon invalid 802 00:50:14,318 --> 00:50:17,599 My case is about cigarettes, and you accused me of adultery and other charges 803 00:50:17,831 --> 00:50:19,603 Just to sentence me to execution 804 00:50:19,628 --> 00:50:25,201 How can you sentence me to execution while I’m blindfolded, shackled, and standing on my feet? 805 00:50:25,847 --> 00:50:27,027 Then one of them stood up 806 00:50:28,120 --> 00:50:29,120 And hit me 807 00:50:30,159 --> 00:50:34,556 He punched me in the stomach and I fell to the ground. They held me as another came from behind and hit me 808 00:50:34,581 --> 00:50:37,204 Of course, I was blindfolded and didn’t know where the blows were coming from 809 00:50:38,081 --> 00:50:42,909 When he hit me, I said, I accept the judgment and law of God Almighty 810 00:50:44,503 --> 00:50:45,899 Then they brought me down 811 00:50:46,603 --> 00:50:49,448 And all I was thinking about during that conversation 812 00:50:50,339 --> 00:50:55,628 When they brought me down, I was wondering where I would go. My friends hadn’t opened the sharaqa [door vent] inside the room 813 00:50:56,300 --> 00:51:00,628 I was surprised when I came down the stairs because I knew the way down to the investigation area 814 00:51:01,182 --> 00:51:05,847 We went straight down, then came to a small landing, turned, and went down again 815 00:51:06,456 --> 00:51:10,509 Right in front of us was the investigation door, leading westward 816 00:51:10,534 --> 00:51:15,119 Suddenly, they took me toward the investigation door, and then 817 00:51:15,870 --> 00:51:17,597 They unlocked the cuffs on my hands 818 00:51:18,323 --> 00:51:21,417 And started shackling my left hand 819 00:51:21,956 --> 00:51:25,526 When they shackled my left hand, I lifted the cloth blindfold from my eyes 820 00:51:25,972 --> 00:51:29,175 I looked and saw that everyone was masked, and at the investigation door 821 00:51:29,323 --> 00:51:33,030 There was a black vehicle for transporting prisoners, marked “Judicial Police” 822 00:51:33,104 --> 00:51:36,589 When they issued execution sentences 823 00:51:37,081 --> 00:51:44,311 They handed the sentenced prisoners to the Judicial Police, which handled executions and amputations 824 00:51:44,917 --> 00:51:46,323 At that moment 825 00:51:46,831 --> 00:51:50,834 Terror, death, and pain fell upon me 826 00:51:50,952 --> 00:51:55,175 It was a situation no one would envy, terrible to the point that 827 00:51:55,191 --> 00:52:01,853 It truly defies description, no matter how I try to explain it 828 00:52:01,885 --> 00:52:07,157 When I saw the Judicial Police and their black vehicle parked there 829 00:52:07,782 --> 00:52:10,782 I saw my friend, Ibrahim al-Fakkash 830 00:52:12,251 --> 00:52:13,909 And another man, both shackled 831 00:52:15,252 --> 00:52:16,509 They hit me immediately 832 00:52:16,774 --> 00:52:20,852 Blindfolded me again, put a black bag over my head, shackled me, and made me sit down 833 00:52:22,626 --> 00:52:28,790 I felt that Ibrahim al-Fakkash, out of fear, was repeating, “There is no god but God” 834 00:52:28,819 --> 00:52:31,714 I crawled toward him 835 00:52:33,204 --> 00:52:35,104 And it was sunset time 836 00:52:36,206 --> 00:52:37,804 I asked him, Are you al-Breiwi? 837 00:52:38,300 --> 00:52:40,089 He said, Yes. I asked, What did they do? 838 00:52:40,272 --> 00:52:41,796 He said, They sentenced me to execution 839 00:52:43,499 --> 00:52:46,858 Then we cried—not because we wanted to, but out of sheer terror 840 00:52:47,202 --> 00:52:51,397 Our fear wasn’t only of death or being shot 841 00:52:51,741 --> 00:52:58,483 But from imagining them holding our heads and slitting our throats with a knife—that’s what truly terrified us 842 00:52:58,530 --> 00:53:03,515 Every second we were dying millions of times inside 843 00:53:04,327 --> 00:53:10,991 Or a billion times—each time imagining the knife descending on our necks 844 00:53:11,108 --> 00:53:15,294 We had already seen ISIS videos showing people being slaughtered with knives 845 00:53:15,319 --> 00:53:22,569 Those scenes stuck in my mind, and I imagined the blade cutting through my neck 846 00:53:23,163 --> 00:53:24,614 We cried from fear 847 00:53:24,639 --> 00:53:29,343 We knew well who ISIS were—they had no mercy, no compassion 848 00:53:30,788 --> 00:53:34,382 The third, the fourth, and others came, but one person remained 849 00:53:35,716 --> 00:53:38,115 It was during Ramadan, and we were fasting 850 00:53:41,014 --> 00:53:44,413 As you know, at sunset, when the call to prayer begins for those fasting 851 00:53:47,755 --> 00:53:49,911 Ibrahim al-Alkan said, I want water 852 00:53:49,935 --> 00:53:54,630 Before you cut off our heads, we ask you to bring us water—we are fasting 853 00:53:59,841 --> 00:54:01,161 So they brought us water 854 00:54:01,841 --> 00:54:03,990 We couldn’t see, because we were blindfolded 855 00:54:04,490 --> 00:54:06,317 They gave the water to Ibrahim 856 00:54:07,395 --> 00:54:12,294 When he drank, the water got stuck in his throat from fear 857 00:54:12,958 --> 00:54:17,129 It lodged in his throat, and he began choking 858 00:54:17,723 --> 00:54:21,692 I was so terrified, I thought they were strangling us one by one 859 00:54:23,270 --> 00:54:27,206 At first, when we heard the sunset call to prayer, we relaxed, thinking no execution would happen then 860 00:54:27,231 --> 00:54:30,216 Because they usually wanted crowds to film their productions 861 00:54:30,716 --> 00:54:35,845 So when the call to prayer came, we felt some relief, knowing that executions never happened at sunset or night 862 00:54:36,434 --> 00:54:38,692 They never carried out executions at that time 863 00:54:40,372 --> 00:54:42,762 When Ibrahim started choking, fear overtook me 864 00:54:43,747 --> 00:54:48,958 I crawled backward and ended up in the arms of my friend al-Breiwi 865 00:54:49,012 --> 00:54:52,005 When I stretched out my right hand, I touched the shoe of one of the ISIS members 866 00:54:52,317 --> 00:54:54,770 Each of us had an ISIS member standing right beside him 867 00:54:56,204 --> 00:55:00,442 I jumped into al-Breiwi’s arms and told him, They’re going to strangle us one by one 868 00:55:01,864 --> 00:55:08,552 Then they brought the last person—I think it was Baraa al-Ano—and shackled him 869 00:55:10,637 --> 00:55:12,212 They put us inside the vehicle 870 00:55:12,755 --> 00:55:18,739 We could see a little from under the blindfold. Inside the vehicle were sharp metal pieces and swords 871 00:55:19,083 --> 00:55:23,381 It felt like we were in a scene from the “Khalid ibn al-Walid” series from the sword era 872 00:55:23,794 --> 00:55:25,200 We just sat there 873 00:55:26,512 --> 00:55:33,512 Since we were locals and knew the streets well from selling cigarettes there 874 00:55:33,614 --> 00:55:36,200 Sometimes we could tell where we were heading 875 00:55:36,645 --> 00:55:39,114 We reached the Euphrates Basin area roughly 876 00:55:40,900 --> 00:55:42,005 Then the road disappeared 877 00:55:43,184 --> 00:55:45,661 They pushed our heads down and spoke to us 878 00:55:47,114 --> 00:55:49,856 There were two escort vehicles alongside the one carrying us 879 00:55:50,966 --> 00:55:54,848 The main vehicle was black, designated for prisoner transport, marked “Judicial Police” 880 00:55:55,198 --> 00:56:00,996 Among the escorts was Abu Khaled al-Idlibi, the Emir of the Islamic Police 881 00:56:02,864 --> 00:56:05,755 They took us to the al-Jazra junction area in Raqqa city 882 00:56:06,403 --> 00:56:10,005 The next day, we learned that we were at the al-Jazra junction 883 00:56:11,606 --> 00:56:16,802 They put us in a small isolated room with only a toilet 884 00:56:17,993 --> 00:56:21,598 A small room with a toilet recently prepared and designated for us 885 00:56:22,348 --> 00:56:25,606 There was a small window in the wall like this 886 00:56:26,907 --> 00:56:29,770 It had thick iron bars 887 00:56:30,411 --> 00:56:35,091 They brought us new iron chains—thick ones, not thin 888 00:56:35,591 --> 00:56:39,169 As if they were meant to restrain some giant beast 889 00:56:39,194 --> 00:56:42,919 Like in American or foreign movies 890 00:56:43,278 --> 00:56:49,542 There was a huge gorilla in one movie that they enlarged with cameras and computers 891 00:56:49,567 --> 00:56:53,098 It felt like they were chaining a giant gorilla with those massive chains 892 00:56:53,591 --> 00:56:56,481 The chains were so thick that the prisoner couldn’t even move his hand 893 00:56:57,270 --> 00:57:02,201 They shackled us with those chains, and between each person there were 26 links 894 00:57:02,841 --> 00:57:04,606 The chains were white and new 895 00:57:05,404 --> 00:57:09,169 Abu Seif told me he had bought them especially for me, of the expensive kind 896 00:57:09,194 --> 00:57:11,169 He said he bought them exclusively from Qoutli Street 897 00:57:11,559 --> 00:57:13,605 They also brought 7 locks 898 00:57:13,630 --> 00:57:17,902 They placed the chain on my hand and locked it with a padlock 899 00:57:17,927 --> 00:57:23,052 Then left 26 links’ space, chained my friend’s hand, and locked it too 900 00:57:23,077 --> 00:57:26,122 They used to bring us a plate of oil and thyme 901 00:57:26,669 --> 00:57:29,106 They would come in wearing masks and bring us food 902 00:57:29,211 --> 00:57:32,450 I used to tell Al-Bariwi, our charge isn’t about the cigarette trade 903 00:57:32,475 --> 00:57:35,362 They chained us and sentenced us to execution 904 00:57:35,387 --> 00:57:39,823 And today this man comes in bringing us food while masked, and we’re bound with iron chains 905 00:57:39,848 --> 00:57:44,652 They even spoke to us through cameras and loudspeakers, telling us to move back 906 00:57:44,677 --> 00:57:51,544 So we’d retreat and hide in a corner of the room while they brought in a plate of oil and thyme 907 00:57:51,950 --> 00:57:57,028 We’d eat like that, and during the first 7 days, we didn’t eat anything until an emir came in 908 00:57:58,130 --> 00:58:00,536 He was from the Al-Dabba family, his name was Abu Saqr 909 00:58:01,661 --> 00:58:05,255 May God grant him wellness, guide him, and forgive him 910 00:58:05,966 --> 00:58:08,434 May God forgive him in this life and the next 911 00:58:08,856 --> 00:58:12,610 He came to us and started sitting with us every day for half an hour 912 00:58:13,130 --> 00:58:15,223 He would tell us prophetic hadiths 913 00:58:15,536 --> 00:58:17,708 May God bless him and guide him 914 00:58:18,442 --> 00:58:20,294 And protect him for his children, God willing 915 00:58:21,333 --> 00:58:27,786 He began forcing us to eat, like how one feeds a patient in intensive care 916 00:58:27,811 --> 00:58:31,762 He’d put a bite of bread in our mouths, forcing us to eat 917 00:58:31,841 --> 00:58:33,919 He started easing our suffering a little 918 00:58:34,934 --> 00:58:38,317 We stayed like this under Abu Saqr’s care 919 00:58:39,692 --> 00:58:41,958 For about three and a half months 920 00:58:43,731 --> 00:58:47,231 Around three and a half months, or about three months and 20 days 921 00:58:47,833 --> 00:58:51,701 When Abu Saqr’s shift ended, another would take over at night 922 00:58:51,958 --> 00:58:55,311 No one dealt with us except Abu Saqr and the plate of thyme 923 00:58:55,770 --> 00:58:56,916 I fell ill 924 00:58:57,503 --> 00:58:59,177 And the weather turned cold 925 00:59:00,333 --> 00:59:04,833 It’s honestly painful to talk about 926 00:59:05,341 --> 00:59:08,005 It wasn’t a human act 927 00:59:08,841 --> 00:59:13,325 Nor an Islamic one, not even something the Jews of Khaybar would do 928 00:59:13,606 --> 00:59:18,317 Because 7 of us sentenced to execution were chained with iron shackles 929 00:59:18,731 --> 00:59:21,262 And there were 27 links between each man 930 00:59:21,544 --> 00:59:23,574 When I needed to go to the toilets 931 00:59:23,599 --> 00:59:25,200 God is witness to what I say 932 00:59:25,536 --> 00:59:28,175 If I wanted to go to the toilets 933 00:59:28,208 --> 00:59:30,402 And I wanted to sit to relieve myself 934 00:59:30,427 --> 00:59:33,817 When I sat, my friend next to me had to bend toward me 935 00:59:34,802 --> 00:59:38,403 The third had to bend his body, and the fourth had to stand over me 936 00:59:38,692 --> 00:59:43,879 Meaning four of us stood in the toilet so one could relieve himself, may God honor you 937 00:59:44,497 --> 00:59:49,270 What kind of Islam is that? What sharia allows it? 938 00:59:49,661 --> 00:59:55,997 Seven Muslim men chained together, entering the toilets three or four at a time 939 00:59:56,419 --> 00:59:57,919 It was disgusting behavior 940 00:59:59,825 --> 01:00:02,317 And when one of us got sick 941 01:00:02,966 --> 01:00:06,692 Many of the young men got diarrhea, or someone needed to urinate, it was obvious 942 01:00:07,075 --> 01:00:12,012 We all had to wake up, because between me and the next man were 26 links, so all 7 of us had to get up 943 01:00:12,698 --> 01:00:16,903 Especially when one of us was sick with diarrhea 944 01:00:19,364 --> 01:00:25,012 We stayed like that for about three and a half months, or three months and 20 days 945 01:00:29,895 --> 01:00:32,708 Our daily routine among ourselves 946 01:00:32,855 --> 01:00:38,520 We had no real routine, only reading the Quran, crying, and supplicating 947 01:00:38,919 --> 01:00:41,825 Asking God to free us and the nation of Muhammad 948 01:00:42,286 --> 01:00:45,409 At night, we’d sit 949 01:00:45,528 --> 01:00:51,786 Reciting the Quran and praying Fajr until sunrise 950 01:00:52,309 --> 01:00:54,731 We’d fall asleep drowsy, still in our chains 951 01:00:55,122 --> 01:01:00,403 We barely slept half an hour or less before waking up in fear—some crying, some deep in thought 952 01:01:00,835 --> 01:01:03,882 Some would sit alone in the corner, imagining death 953 01:01:03,907 --> 01:01:08,833 When they were about to execute one or a group, the organization would film an ISIS video 954 01:01:09,153 --> 01:01:10,524 On a disc or flash drive 955 01:01:10,880 --> 01:01:14,506 Abu Seif brought us a flash drive back then, and we watched it 956 01:01:14,661 --> 01:01:19,503 It was like horror movies; we were sentenced to death, and he made us watch people being beheaded 957 01:01:19,528 --> 01:01:22,028 May God not grant him success in this world or the next 958 01:01:22,059 --> 01:01:25,777 He showed us footage of an execution slaughterhouse in Deir ez-Zor Province 959 01:01:25,802 --> 01:01:29,034 Where they executed 35 people hanging from sharp iron hooks 960 01:01:29,059 --> 01:01:36,239 One emir was carrying two men like this, wearing a short white robe—he was from Deir ez-Zor 961 01:01:36,506 --> 01:01:37,706 He carried two men like that 962 01:01:38,786 --> 01:01:41,388 He killed them and hung them on iron hooks 963 01:01:41,466 --> 01:01:43,208 Then Mustafa, known as Abu Seif 964 01:01:43,778 --> 01:01:47,809 Said to me, “Look, Talal al-Shuweimi, do you see the execution prep, Talal al-Shuweimi?” 965 01:01:48,598 --> 01:01:49,833 And I was under a death sentence 966 01:01:49,942 --> 01:01:57,380 Sometimes the hallucinations and fear cause sicknesses like diarrhea, chills, and psychological illness 967 01:01:57,630 --> 01:02:00,130 There was a man with us named Qadri al-Dabba 968 01:02:00,302 --> 01:02:02,966 Qadri al-Dabba started screaming in his sleep 969 01:02:04,395 --> 01:02:11,341 His eyes bulged, foam came out of his mouth—he became seriously ill, not normal at all 970 01:02:12,247 --> 01:02:16,362 Interviewer: Did the organization help him, or did they leave him to suffer? 971 01:02:16,387 --> 01:02:22,656 They didn’t give him anything; his fits would come every 4 or 5 hours 972 01:02:22,680 --> 01:02:24,919 One time, I fell sick myself 973 01:02:25,458 --> 01:02:29,028 I got chills and severe diarrhea inside the prison 974 01:02:29,567 --> 01:02:34,692 It knocked me to the ground, and I was near death 975 01:02:35,528 --> 01:02:37,387 By God’s power, I recovered 976 01:02:37,412 --> 01:02:41,731 My friends spoke to the ISIS members, but they didn’t give me injections or any help 977 01:02:41,756 --> 01:02:45,356 That happened during the days I was sentenced to death 978 01:02:45,606 --> 01:02:49,042 I don’t know if my illness came from fear or was a real sickness 979 01:02:49,067 --> 01:02:53,677 When I fell ill, there was a jailer under Abu Seif named Abu Abdul Malik 980 01:02:53,825 --> 01:02:56,239 He was from Tabqa, originally from Aleppo Province 981 01:02:57,216 --> 01:03:01,942 He watched us through the cameras; even Ibrahim al-Fakkash started crying over me 982 01:03:02,356 --> 01:03:08,208 And Ibrahim al-Alkan recited Quran over me, rubbing my body and weeping, thinking I was dying 983 01:03:10,489 --> 01:03:17,583 And Ibrahim al-Ayyoub, nicknamed Shantar, was a simple, impatient man 984 01:03:17,887 --> 01:03:21,583 He stood up, started smashing the door, shouting, “Talal al-Shuweimi is dead!” 985 01:03:21,950 --> 01:03:24,106 Abu Abdul Malik answered him 986 01:03:25,052 --> 01:03:26,317 And said, when he dies 987 01:03:26,817 --> 01:03:30,692 When he dies, let us know so we can take him out—that’s what Abu Abdul Malik said 988 01:03:31,411 --> 01:03:35,137 Praise be to God, Talal al-Shuweimi is still alive by God’s grace 989 01:03:35,162 --> 01:03:39,317 Abu Abdul Malik was later struck by an airstrike on his way to Tabqa 990 01:03:40,442 --> 01:03:43,481 May God have mercy on him—I recited Al-Fatiha for him 991 01:03:47,905 --> 01:03:49,997 There were mediations everywhere 992 01:03:50,326 --> 01:03:54,997 But not before God Almighty—there’s no mediation, and no bribery works 993 01:03:56,059 --> 01:04:01,895 When I was sentenced to death, neither my mother, my father, nor anyone could help me 994 01:04:01,920 --> 01:04:04,497 Only God Almighty can help you 995 01:04:05,419 --> 01:04:08,692 And your deeds help you in their eyes 996 01:04:08,803 --> 01:04:10,464 But they still had mediations 997 01:04:10,489 --> 01:04:13,778 One of my neighbors was detained 998 01:04:14,614 --> 01:04:19,825 His name was Qusai Ahmad al-Omara—he paid them 5,000 dollars and was released 999 01:04:20,101 --> 01:04:21,411 In the final days, for example 1000 01:04:22,427 --> 01:04:26,184 I was under the death sentence when the judicial committee arrived 1001 01:04:26,817 --> 01:04:28,294 They started bringing me my wife 1002 01:04:29,153 --> 01:04:31,115 Then they started bringing me my second wife 1003 01:04:31,262 --> 01:04:34,177 Of course, in exchange for money, whoever wanted the price of a motorcycle, I gave it to him 1004 01:04:34,202 --> 01:04:36,692 And whoever wanted diesel for his house, I gave it to him 1005 01:04:37,200 --> 01:04:43,044 There were many things I benefited from after my family found out that I was in prison 1006 01:04:43,692 --> 01:04:45,606 We started playing openly 1007 01:04:46,028 --> 01:04:48,411 When my family found out that I was in prison 1008 01:04:48,630 --> 01:04:50,239 My wives started 1009 01:04:50,607 --> 01:04:51,607 And even 1010 01:04:52,607 --> 01:04:57,075 Almost every day they came to visit me, one day they came, one day they didn’t 1011 01:04:57,286 --> 01:04:59,848 Of course secretly, so no one would see us outside 1012 01:04:59,873 --> 01:05:03,067 The prison was in a narrow corner facing my family’s house, and I saw them secretly 1013 01:05:03,092 --> 01:05:07,317 They brought silver and gold coins, and their food was now paid for by us personally 1014 01:05:07,950 --> 01:05:10,731 We were 7 people sentenced to execution 1015 01:05:11,247 --> 01:05:16,618 Every day one of us paid for the food, the seven of us, and this prison 1016 01:05:17,028 --> 01:05:25,026 It seemed that the jailers, the emir, and the rest of the group bringing food were all from poor families 1017 01:05:25,106 --> 01:05:27,299 When they brought food, they gave it to the other prisoners 1018 01:05:27,324 --> 01:05:31,072 Because the prison had two sections: one for the rest of the prisoners and one just for us seven 1019 01:05:31,104 --> 01:05:35,854 We seven took turns. For example, if it was my turn, my wife knew that today was my turn 1020 01:05:36,198 --> 01:05:42,024 She brought food for me and for the guards, like fried chicken one day and grilled kebab another 1021 01:05:42,547 --> 01:05:48,930 She brought us ready meals from the market, and there was plenty. My turn came once a week since we were 7 people 1022 01:05:48,955 --> 01:05:51,923 Every day food was paid for by one of us, so my turn came once a week to bring the food 1023 01:05:51,948 --> 01:05:57,213 The food bill was about 75 to 100 thousand per month, all ready food from the market for us 1024 01:05:57,393 --> 01:06:00,704 And the ISIS members started sitting and spending evenings with us 1025 01:06:00,729 --> 01:06:04,988 They chatted with us, and we bought nuts and soft drinks, though we were shackled 1026 01:06:05,690 --> 01:06:08,776 They trusted us and learned our story 1027 01:06:08,987 --> 01:06:12,963 They told us, “Your charge is collaboration with the Global Coalition, and you’re sentenced to execution” 1028 01:06:13,065 --> 01:06:16,182 That actually helped us a lot in planning our escape 1029 01:06:16,383 --> 01:06:23,408 Interviewer: Didn’t you try, for example, to pay them money to get out of prison? 1030 01:06:23,815 --> 01:06:28,002 Interviewer: Many people paid and got released, even those sentenced to execution 1031 01:06:28,027 --> 01:06:31,971 I was well known, one of the familiar figures 1032 01:06:32,135 --> 01:06:36,877 I was chained with iron shackles, and it was impossible for me to pay money 1033 01:06:37,315 --> 01:06:44,189 To escape, break free from the chains, and leave the room. There were 3 locked iron doors 1034 01:06:44,354 --> 01:06:49,518 But once, we had a jailer who wasn’t just a jailer—he was also an emir in ISIS 1035 01:06:49,846 --> 01:06:51,744 In the Mafarq al-Jazra area 1036 01:06:53,112 --> 01:06:58,531 They said he was one of the “Kanas” family. I remember only his nickname, I forgot his name, but he was a good man too 1037 01:06:58,846 --> 01:07:02,007 In the morning, the emir Abu Saqr from the al-Dabba family came 1038 01:07:02,158 --> 01:07:03,990 He was on morning duty as the emir 1039 01:07:04,221 --> 01:07:08,371 At night, the shift changed. That man left around sunset, and another took over 1040 01:07:08,396 --> 01:07:10,471 He was from Mafarq al-Jazra and was well known 1041 01:07:10,721 --> 01:07:12,963 He told me he was married to two women 1042 01:07:14,026 --> 01:07:20,416 Sometimes he came and sat with us, but ISIS put him under surveillance because he was kind and sympathetic to prisoners 1043 01:07:21,783 --> 01:07:26,120 We paid him 7 million to bring us just a piece of a saw 1044 01:07:26,512 --> 01:07:28,003 So we could escape, we told him 1045 01:07:28,347 --> 01:07:32,244 We’re 7 prisoners. If you can just bring us a small metal saw blade 1046 01:07:32,643 --> 01:07:37,502 Just so we could cut the big lock hanging on the wall 1047 01:07:39,539 --> 01:07:40,682 To escape 1048 01:07:40,707 --> 01:07:45,760 We told him each of us would give you one million. He said, “ISIS will kill me.” We said, “You’ll escape with us” 1049 01:07:46,307 --> 01:07:50,612 Praise God, he was supposed to bring us the saw blade 1050 01:07:50,924 --> 01:07:55,002 But the next day, they arrested him 1051 01:07:55,330 --> 01:07:59,688 They transferred him. We didn’t know whether he was detained or relocated, but we never saw him again after that 1052 01:08:00,208 --> 01:08:01,807 And we never learned what happened 1053 01:08:06,838 --> 01:08:08,487 When they tried to bargain with me 1054 01:08:09,244 --> 01:08:12,019 They bargained with me a lot, even the well-known emirs 1055 01:08:12,877 --> 01:08:14,526 There were prominent figures among them 1056 01:08:15,901 --> 01:08:22,282 Specifically, one from the al-Amer family and one from the al-Mukhtar family bargained with me 1057 01:08:22,941 --> 01:08:25,641 They told me, “Bring us fertilizer” 1058 01:08:25,938 --> 01:08:32,196 Fertilizer 33, the one they use to make explosives 1059 01:08:32,860 --> 01:08:37,110 They bargained with me to bring cigarettes and hide the fertilizer inside them for them 1060 01:08:37,282 --> 01:08:41,297 Many people bargained with me, not just them 1061 01:08:41,547 --> 01:08:47,501 Even Abu Seif himself bargained with me to work with him 1062 01:08:47,977 --> 01:08:51,416 And to pass on reports and information to him 1063 01:08:51,968 --> 01:08:54,233 He told me, “Whatever you want in return, you’ll get it” 1064 01:08:54,258 --> 01:08:57,704 I told him, “I’m not that kind of man” 1065 01:08:58,102 --> 01:09:01,697 That’s when Abu Seif began to hate me 1066 01:09:02,540 --> 01:09:05,204 His eyes turned red with anger toward me 1067 01:09:06,596 --> 01:09:07,796 And one time 1068 01:09:10,149 --> 01:09:12,111 There’s a story I want to tell 1069 01:09:13,385 --> 01:09:17,594 ISIS planted a man with disabled children and placed him next to me 1070 01:09:18,617 --> 01:09:23,604 Pretending he was wanted by ISIS for a rifle he couldn’t afford, and he had disabled children 1071 01:09:23,828 --> 01:09:26,977 He lived next to me and became very close to me 1072 01:09:28,266 --> 01:09:31,508 He grew close like one of my sons, and I pitied him 1073 01:09:32,522 --> 01:09:36,974 Even my family felt sorry for him and cried, and we bought him things for his home 1074 01:09:37,086 --> 01:09:40,969 When my family went shopping for bread or vegetables 1075 01:09:40,994 --> 01:09:43,203 They bought things for his kids like for ours 1076 01:09:43,227 --> 01:09:46,602 I told my wife, “That man’s home is like a second home to me” 1077 01:09:46,750 --> 01:09:48,687 Because he was sick and unemployed 1078 01:09:49,352 --> 01:09:54,453 His family did nothing, and he had disabled children 1079 01:09:54,478 --> 01:09:57,553 He spent about a month and a half trying to work with me 1080 01:09:57,578 --> 01:10:00,781 I told him, “Stay home. I’ll cover all your kids’ expenses” 1081 01:10:00,789 --> 01:10:01,804 One time 1082 01:10:03,726 --> 01:10:06,945 I was sitting on the roof smoking a waterpipe 1083 01:10:07,734 --> 01:10:12,718 I saw the man collapse unconscious. My brother was with me; we carried him to the hospital 1084 01:10:14,313 --> 01:10:19,804 Apparently, he had a neurological illness. I didn’t know what it was; I thought he was dead 1085 01:10:20,399 --> 01:10:24,945 He knew I traded in cigarettes but didn’t know where the main warehouses were 1086 01:10:25,492 --> 01:10:30,538 When he saw my kindness and my faith, he loved me and refused to betray me 1087 01:10:31,280 --> 01:10:32,648 I took him to the hospital 1088 01:10:33,046 --> 01:10:34,600 In less than 10 minutes 1089 01:10:37,749 --> 01:10:41,874 All the emirs in the province came—at least the ones I knew 1090 01:10:42,319 --> 01:10:45,130 They had very long beards, though mine reached my navel 1091 01:10:45,155 --> 01:10:47,288 But mine was nothing compared to theirs 1092 01:10:47,507 --> 01:10:52,116 They were all emirs, migrants from Iraq 1093 01:10:52,765 --> 01:11:00,209 Migrants from various nationalities, including well-known migrant women and high-ranking emirs I never imagined 1094 01:11:00,608 --> 01:11:02,804 They came to check on him. At that moment, I 1095 01:11:03,210 --> 01:11:05,388 Was leaning against the wall, then I went down 1096 01:11:05,413 --> 01:11:08,677 I collapsed from fear; my legs couldn’t hold me 1097 01:11:08,702 --> 01:11:12,482 I sat on the floor like that. My life was ruined 1098 01:11:12,507 --> 01:11:14,709 Not just ruined—they were going to kill me too 1099 01:11:14,734 --> 01:11:16,669 For me, I was already finished 1100 01:11:16,694 --> 01:11:19,734 I sat there and wanted to stand up, but I couldn’t 1101 01:11:20,444 --> 01:11:23,109 They came to me and asked about the man’s condition 1102 01:11:23,134 --> 01:11:27,523 From time to time, someone would come to me. All of them had long beards and held high positions 1103 01:11:29,343 --> 01:11:31,804 When I saw the emirs 1104 01:11:33,937 --> 01:11:35,359 My tears started to fall 1105 01:11:37,163 --> 01:11:44,062 The man, who was in intensive care, turned his face toward me and looked at me. I wiped my tears like this, and he saw me 1106 01:11:46,648 --> 01:11:48,484 He gestured to his wife 1107 01:11:50,663 --> 01:11:52,530 He pointed at me, so his wife came over 1108 01:11:54,562 --> 01:11:56,624 She said, “What’s wrong? Why are you crying?” 1109 01:11:57,030 --> 01:12:00,015 I told her quietly, “Did I do something to you?” 1110 01:12:00,499 --> 01:12:03,562 My voice wouldn’t come out because I was completely exhausted inside the hospital 1111 01:12:04,577 --> 01:12:07,109 I said, “Please, I beg you, help me get out of the room” 1112 01:12:07,599 --> 01:12:09,069 When I stood up like this 1113 01:12:10,217 --> 01:12:11,905 The Emir of the Islamic Police arrived 1114 01:12:11,930 --> 01:12:16,710 His base was in the Mefraq al-Jazra area, and his name was Muhammad Tawhid, known as Abu Tawhid 1115 01:12:16,735 --> 01:12:20,650 He had a black SUV, like Murad Alamdar’s car from the Turkish series 1116 01:12:20,675 --> 01:12:24,640 Everyone knew that when Muhammad Tawhid arrived, it was as if Murad Alamdar himself had come 1117 01:12:25,124 --> 01:12:26,765 Then Muhammad Tawhid came 1118 01:12:27,554 --> 01:12:29,648 When I stood up, he looked at me 1119 01:12:30,359 --> 01:12:32,089 He said, “Welcome, welcome” 1120 01:12:32,577 --> 01:12:34,405 “Welcome, Talal al-Shuweimi” 1121 01:12:34,632 --> 01:12:39,898 I knew him, so I said softly, “Sheikh, do you know me?” I was honestly terrified 1122 01:12:41,515 --> 01:12:42,703 He asked, “Why are you crying?” 1123 01:12:42,985 --> 01:12:44,665 I said, “Because my friend is sick” 1124 01:12:45,156 --> 01:12:49,262 I told him that, hoping it would give me a chance to leave the room 1125 01:12:50,726 --> 01:12:52,609 He said, “Is there anyone who doesn’t know you?” 1126 01:12:55,937 --> 01:12:59,112 “You’re the biggest dealer of vice in town.
